confirmed.title: Slack assistant streaming calls coroutine AsyncWebClient.chat_stream un-awaited -- both streaming paths raise AttributeError
confirmed.claim: In the live Bolt AsyncApp assistant path, `client` is an AsyncWebClient whose `chat_stream` is a coroutine function returning AsyncChatStream (append/stop also coroutines), but _stream_simple_response (:177) and _stream_complex_task_plan (:203) call `client.chat_stream(...)`, `streamer.append(...)`, and `streamer.stop()` synchronously -- so `streamer` is an un-awaited coroutine object and `.append` raises AttributeError, meaning every non-DIRECT Slack assistant message dies into the broad except at :141-149 and the user gets 'Something went wrong'.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/slack_bot/streaming_integration.py:177-189:
    streamer = client.chat_stream(
        channel=channel_id,
        recipient_team_id=team_id,
        recipient_user_id=user_id,
        thread_ts=thread_ts,
    )
    for chunk in _split_chunks(full, 80):
        streamer.append(markdown_text=chunk)
...
    streamer.stop()
Registration proves async client: backend/slack_bot/assistant_lifecycle.py:194-197 `@assistant.user_message / async def on_user_message(body, client, say, set_status, logger): await handler.handle_user_message(body=body, client=client, ...)` (AsyncApp + AsyncAssistant inject AsyncWebClient). Empirical gate on the live venv (slack_sdk 3.41.0): `inspect.iscoroutinefunction(AsyncWebClient.chat_stream)` -> True; return annotation AsyncChatStream; `AsyncChatStream.append` coroutine -> True, `.stop` coroutine -> True.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T8 rule 4 (empirical gate: runnable evidence attached) + S6 Slack Bolt async docs (https://docs.slack.dev/tools/bolt-python/concepts/socket-mode/ -- async listeners must await async client calls); Python docs on never-awaited coroutines (https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-dev.html#detect-never-awaited-coroutines). Not covered by phase-69 register (its slack_bot rows are commands.py:295, formatters.py:247, _production_fns, scheduler.py) nor by 74.2 (which targets the un-imported assistant_handler.py, not streaming_integration).
confirmed.suggested_fix: In both call sites: `streamer = await client.chat_stream(...)`, `await streamer.append(...)`, `await streamer.stop(...)` (drop the sync-era assumptions); add a smoke test that drives on_user_message with a stub AsyncWebClient asserting no un-awaited coroutines (python -W error::RuntimeWarning).
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in-context: app.py builds AsyncApp and registers AsyncAssistant handlers, so the injected client is AsyncWebClient. Empirical check on the live .venv (slack_sdk 3.41.0) confirms AsyncWebClient.chat_stream is a coroutine function returning AsyncChatStream, whose append/stop are also coroutines. streaming_integration.py:177 and :203 call chat_stream un-awaited, so streamer is a coroutine object and streamer.append() raises AttributeError; both SIMPLE and COMPLEX paths die into the broad except at :141-149 after the full MAS/LLM flow has already run (:159), and the user gets the generic error. No wrapper/guard neutralizes it; not fixed; no mention of streaming_integration or chat_stream in phase-69/70-73 registers or confirmed_findings.json (74.2 targets the separate un-imported assistant_handler.py). P1 stands: live operator-facing feature broken with wasted LLM spend, but contained and not a money-path/security defect.

confirmed.title: async _stream_complex_task_plan blocks the Slack bot event loop on concurrent.futures.as_completed/future.result
confirmed.claim: Inside `async def _stream_complex_task_plan`, agent fan-out uses a ThreadPoolExecutor but then iterates `for future in as_completed(futures)` and calls `future.result()` -- concurrent.futures.as_completed is a BLOCKING iterator, so the bot's single event loop (queue processor, SLA monitor, stuck-task reaper, all other Slack handlers, Socket Mode ping/pong) freezes for the multi-second duration of each Claude agent call; latent behind pysvc-01 (currently unreachable because chat_stream crashes first at :203), it bites immediately once pysvc-01 is fixed.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T1 rule 1 / source #1 (https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/async/ -- blocking calls inside async def 'block the server'; same rule applies to any asyncio app) + S6 Bolt Socket Mode guidance. Distinct from phase-69 register's orchestrator.py:1810 (backend/agents scope) and from its slack_bot rows.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace with `tasks = [asyncio.create_task(asyncio.to_thread(_run_agent, at)) for at in agents]` + `for coro in asyncio.as_completed(tasks): agent_type, result = await coro` (or loop.run_in_executor + asyncio.wait), keeping the per-agent streamed task-card updates.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in code: streaming_integration.py:193 is `async def _stream_complex_task_plan`; line 21 imports concurrent.futures.as_completed (blocking, not asyncio's); lines 257-267 iterate `as_completed(futures)` + `future.result()` exactly as quoted. The chain runs unshielded on the bot's single event loop: app.py:29 AsyncApp + AsyncSocketModeHandler, assistant_lifecycle.py:153 awaits handle_user_message_with_streaming, which awaits _stream_complex_task_plan at :129 — no to_thread/run_in_executor anywhere. Each agent runs call_single_agent_sync (multi_agent_orchestrator.py:388) -> _call_agent_with_tools, a synchronous multi-turn LLM tool loop (multi-second), so the coroutine blocks the loop inside as_completed's wait, freezing Socket Mode ping/pong, queue processor, SLA monitor, and all other handlers until the slowest agent completes. Not a duplicate: phase-69 register's blocking-I/O row is backend/agents/orchestrator.py:1810 (different file/scope) and its slack_bot rows cover commands/formatters/jobs/scheduler only; no register or confirmed_findings entry touches streaming_integration.py. Latent behind pysvc-01 (client.chat_stream at :203 precedes it in the same function), so P2 (latent debt that becomes a live whole-bot freeze once pysvc-01 is fixed) is honest. Suggested asyncio.to_thread + asyncio.as_completed fix is correct and preserves per-agent task-card streaming.

confirmed.title: Logging formatter selection inverted: production gets ANSI-colored compact logs, debug gets the JSON meant for production
confirmed.claim: setup_logging's branch contradicts its own comment: `settings.debug` (default False per backend/config/settings.py) selects JsonFormatter, so the default/production process emits ANSI-color CompactFormatter lines into backend.log (tee'd by launchd) -- structured JSON logging never runs in production, log parsers/redaction-audit greps ingest escape codes, and enabling debug flips you to un-colored JSON in the terminal.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/main.py:96-100:
    # Use compact format for local dev, JSON for production
    if settings.debug:
        handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
    else:
        handler.setFormatter(CompactFormatter())
(JsonFormatter docstring at :59: 'Structured JSON logs for production / Cloud Logging.')
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md Internal recon seed ('backend/main.py:~100 -- logging formatter selection appears INVERTED vs its own comment... Hand to backend auditor as a seed') + S7 structlog logging best practices (JSON-in-prod, https://www.structlog.org/en/stable/logging-best-practices.html). Verified in-file this session.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Swap the branches (`if settings.debug: CompactFormatter() else: JsonFormatter()`), or key off an explicit `settings.environment`/TTY check; confirm SecretRedactionFilter patterns still match the JSON-shaped records.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verbatim confirmed at backend/main.py:96-100: comment says compact-for-dev/JSON-for-prod but the branch gives JsonFormatter to settings.debug=True and CompactFormatter to the default path (debug defaults False at settings.py:20). No guard neutralizes it, and CompactFormatter's claimed non-TTY plain fallback (:43 docstring) is not implemented — format() at :47-56 colors unconditionally. Live impact proven: backend.log (84 MB, written today) tail shows ANSI escapes (^[[36m...^[[0m) on every line, so structured JSON logging never runs in production. Not a duplicate: absent from audit_phase69 registers and confirmed_findings.json; only prior mention is the contract_75.0.md recon seed that spawned this audit. P2 honest: SecretRedactionFilter is a handler filter (runs pre-format), so redaction is unaffected — damage is log parseability/audit-grep debt, not live correctness or security.

confirmed.title: /docs, /openapi.json, /redoc served unauthenticated with no conditional-OpenAPI gate
confirmed.claim: The FastAPI app (main.py:389) keeps default docs URLs and _PUBLIC_PATHS explicitly exempts /docs, /openapi.json, and /redoc from the Bearer-auth middleware with no environment gate, so the full API schema (120+ routes incl. trade/flatten/kill-switch endpoints) is enumerable by anything that can reach the port -- Tailscale-bound deployment mitigates exposure but the official guidance is to disable docs outside local/staging via a settings flag.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/main.py:389: `app = FastAPI(` (no docs_url/openapi_url conditioning anywhere in the ctor at :389-394);
backend/main.py:406,420-422:
_PUBLIC_PATHS = (
    ...
    "/docs",
    "/openapi.json",
    "/redoc",
)
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T1 rule 7 + S1 (official Conditional OpenAPI doc, https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/how-to/conditional-openapi/) and A1 charter row ('main.py formatter inversion seed; conditional-OpenAPI'). Boundary note: the broader _PUBLIC_PATHS-vs-security.md drift (health/changelog/signals/sovereign etc.) is A4's item per the brief; this finding is scoped to the docs-exposure FastAPI practice only.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add `openapi_url: str | None` to Settings (None in prod default); pass `openapi_url=settings.openapi_url, docs_url=..., redoc_url=...` to FastAPI() and drop the three entries from _PUBLIC_PATHS, or gate them on settings.debug.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in context: FastAPI ctor at backend/main.py:389-394 has no docs_url/openapi_url/redoc_url conditioning, _PUBLIC_PATHS (:420-422) exempts /docs, /openapi.json, /redoc from the auth middleware, repo-wide grep confirms no gate exists anywhere, and scripts/start_services.sh:18 binds uvicorn to 0.0.0.0:8000, so the full 120+ route schema is enumerable unauthenticated on every host interface. Not neutralized (routes stay auth-gated, but schema disclosure is real). Not a duplicate: phase-69/70/71/72/73 registers contain no conditional-OpenAPI item; the _PUBLIC_PATHS-vs-security.md drift is A4's separately-scoped sibling in this same audit. The exposure IS documented in .claude/rules/security.md ('Auth middleware skips: ... /docs, /openapi.json, /redoc'), so it is convention rather than accident — but the 75.0 brief (T1 rule 7 + A1 charter) explicitly targets this as a practice gap, so it is queued-for-finding, not intentional-and-endorsed. P3 is the honest severity given Tailscale/LAN deployment, info-disclosure-only impact, and one-arg fix; fixer must also update security.md's skip list to avoid worsening A4's doc drift.

confirmed.title: Lifespan shutdown only stops one of two APScheduler instances; paper-trading scheduler, Slack monitor, and prewarm task are never shut down
confirmed.claim: The lifespan finally-block shuts down only `queue_scheduler` (wait=False); the paper-trading AsyncIOScheduler started at :270-274 (which owns the daily trading cron and the harness self-audit cron), the Slack monitor from :290-303, and the prewarm task are never stopped/cancelled -- on reload/SIGTERM, in-flight jobs are killed mid-BQ-write with no orderly drain, and any future multi-app-instance/test harness reusing lifespan leaks running schedulers.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/main.py:377-386:
    finally:
        # Shutdown
        logging.info("PyFinAgent backend shutting down")
        
        # Stop queue processor scheduler if it was started
        try:
            if 'queue_scheduler' in locals():
                queue_scheduler.shutdown(wait=False)
        except Exception:
            pass
(paper scheduler started at :270 `scheduler = AsyncIOScheduler(); init_scheduler(scheduler); scheduler.start()` -- no matching shutdown anywhere in main.py.)
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md S5 (APScheduler FAQ shutdown pitfalls, https://apscheduler.readthedocs.io/en/3.x/faq.html) + FastAPI lifespan pattern (cleanup after yield, https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/events/). The A7 role owns launchd/scripts scheduling; this is the in-code lifespan seam in A1 scope.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Keep references to both schedulers (and the prewarm task) in lifespan scope; in finally: `scheduler.shutdown(wait=False)`, `queue_scheduler.shutdown(wait=False)`, cancel+await the prewarm task, and stop the Slack monitor if it exposes a close hook.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in backend/main.py: the finally-block (377-386) shuts down only queue_scheduler; the paper-trading AsyncIOScheduler started at :266-271 (daily trading cron + phase-71.6 self-audit cron) has no shutdown call anywhere in the file, the Slack monitor's existing stop() hook (backend/slack_monitor.py:88) is never invoked, and the prewarm task (:373) is never cancelled. Not a duplicate: phase-69 register and confirmed_findings.json only contain a different scheduler finding (paper_trading_hour not re-read by _add_scheduler_job). P3 stands: in single-process production the OS reaps everything on SIGTERM, and the suggested wait=False shutdown would not itself give an orderly drain, so the real exposure is cleanup hygiene (unused stop() hook, leaks under in-process lifespan reuse/tests) -- polish-grade. Minor evidence drift: scheduler start is at :266-271, not :270-274; anchor line 377 is correct.

confirmed.title: MASEventBus: Python-3.14-broken event-loop guard, dead _lock attribute, and thread-per-event HTTP relay
confirmed.claim: MASEventBus.__init__ guards lock creation with `asyncio.get_event_loop().is_running()`, but on Python 3.14 (the repo runtime, 3.14.4) `asyncio.get_event_loop()` raises RuntimeError when there is no current event loop instead of returning a fresh loop, so the `else None` fallback the author wrote for exactly the no-loop case is unreachable without an exception — first construction of the singleton from any sync context (script, REPL, sync service, executor thread) crashes. Compounding: `self._lock` is assigned here and never referenced anywhere else in the module (dead, unfinished thread-safety), and `_forward_remote` spawns a brand-new daemon Thread + fresh httpx connection per emitted event in the Slack-bot relay path.
confirmed.evidence: backend/agents/mas_events.py:97 `self._lock = asyncio.Lock() if asyncio.get_event_loop().is_running() else None` — rg '_lock' over the module returns ONLY line 97 (no reader). backend/agents/mas_events.py:132-144: `def _forward_remote(self, event: MASEvent) -> None:` ... `def _send(): ... httpx.post(f"{self.remote_url}/api/mas/events/ingest", json=asdict(event), timeout=3.0,)` ... `threading.Thread(target=_send, daemon=True).start()`. Also mas_events.py:205 `raw = f"{time.time()}-{id(asyncio.get_event_loop)}"` takes the id of the FUNCTION OBJECT (constant per process) as run-id entropy. Reachability today: all current get_event_bus() first-touches (multi_agent_orchestrator.py:431/588/1336, api/mas_events.py, slack_bot/app_home.py async handlers under AsyncApp) are in-loop, so the crash is latent, not live.
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T2 / source #8 (https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.14.html): Python 3.14 removed implicit event-loop creation — asyncio.get_event_loop raises RuntimeError with no current loop (deprecated since 3.12); brief confirms repo runs Python 3.14.4 and flags the 3.14 asyncio-policy deprecation class as audit surface.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the guard with `try: asyncio.get_running_loop(); self._lock = asyncio.Lock()\nexcept RuntimeError: self._lock = None` — or simply delete `_lock` since nothing uses it. In `_forward_remote`, replace thread-per-event with a single daemon worker draining a queue.Queue through one shared httpx.Client. In make_run_id, drop the meaningless `id(asyncio.get_event_loop)` term (use uuid4 or os.urandom).
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified at mas_events.py:97 on runtime Python 3.14.4 (.venv/bin/python3.14): asyncio.get_event_loop() raises RuntimeError with no current loop in 3.14, making the `else None` fallback unreachable — first singleton construction from any loop-less sync context crashes. _lock appears only on line 97 (dead attribute; guard is also pointless since 3.10 Lock no longer binds a loop). _forward_remote (132-144) confirmed thread-per-event + fresh httpx.post; make_run_id:205 confirmed uses id of the function object (process-constant) as entropy. All current get_event_bus() first-touches are in-loop so the crash is latent, matching P2. No duplicate in confirmed_findings.json, harness_proposals.json, or phase-69/73 audit registers.

confirmed.title: Backtest cache hardcodes `AND market = 'US'` and drops its dead `market` param while the engine, selector, ingestion and API are market-aware
confirmed.claim: preload_prices/preload_fundamentals accept a `market` parameter that is never used, and the price query hardcodes `AND market = 'US'`, while the surrounding layers already shipped multi-market support: the engine stores self.market and fetches a per-market universe (backtest_engine.py:283) and per-market benchmark (:305), data_ingestion writes market='KR'/'EU' rows, and api/backtest.py does per-market FX conversion (phase-50.5). The engine's preload call (:306) additionally omits `market` entirely. First non-US run will preload zero rows (a logged warning only) and execute a silent zero-trade backtest with a flat NAV presented as a valid result. Latent today: all three BacktestEngine constructor sites (api/backtest.py:895, :1014, mcp_servers/backtest_server.py:108) use the default market='US'.
confirmed.evidence: backend/backtest/cache.py:78 `def preload_prices(tickers: list[str], start_date: str, end_date: str, market: str = _DEFAULT_MARKET) -> int:` ... cache.py:100-106 query: `SELECT ticker, date, open, high, low, close, volume, market FROM \`{_table("historical_prices")}\` WHERE ticker IN UNNEST(@tickers) AND date >= @start AND date <= @end AND market = 'US'` (docstring: "Phase 2.9: market param accepted but not filtered yet"). backend/backtest/backtest_engine.py:283 `universe_tickers = self.candidate_selector.get_universe_tickers(market=self.market)`; :305-307 `_benchmark = get_market_config(self.market).get("benchmark", "SPY")` / `cache.preload_prices(universe_tickers + [_benchmark], global_start, global_end)` / `cache.preload_fundamentals(universe_tickers)` — no market passed. backend/backtest/data_ingestion.py:144-153 extracts market from ticker namespace and writes `"market": market` rows.
confirmed.basis: Charter copy-paste-divergence + dead-parameter classes; research_brief_75.0.md internal recon documents phase-50.x market expansion as shipped code (per-market benchmark + FX at api/backtest.py:940-942). Distinct from the excluded phase-69 register row backtest_engine.py:488 (weekend/holiday empty-lookup mechanism) and from excluded roadmap item '5.x market expansion' — this is divergence between already-shipped layers, not feature absence.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Thread self.market through: `cache.preload_prices(..., market=self.market)` (and fundamentals), parameterize the WHERE with `AND market = @market`; until then, make preload_prices raise (or loudly warn + fall back) when market != 'US' instead of silently filtering the universe to zero rows.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified line-by-line: preload_prices (cache.py:78) and preload_fundamentals (:135) accept a `market` param that is never used, the price query hardcodes AND market = 'US' (:105), and backtest_engine.py:306-307 omits market from both preload calls despite being market-aware three lines earlier (market-aware universe :283, per-market benchmark :305) — genuine divergence left behind by phase-50.5, which shipped market support in the engine, selector, ingestion, and API FX layer (api/backtest.py:942) but not the cache. Reachability is latent as claimed (all API/MCP/meta_coordinator constructor sites default market='US'), though rotation_runner.py:134 already threads market=p.get("market","US") from strategy params, so a non-US run is one config key away. One correction to the failure scenario: a non-US run would NOT be a silent zero-trade/flat-NAV backtest — with zero preloaded rows, candidate_selector.py:42-47 falls back to scanning the full universe via cached_prices' per-ticker BQ fallback (cache.py:303-307), whose query has no market filter, so data loads via the ~50k-individual-query path the cache exists to prevent, with 30s timeouts silently yielding empty frames (severe slowdown + partial data, not flat NAV). Not a duplicate: the phase-69 register item on cache.py is the weekend/holiday empty-slice mechanism (cached_prices :282-292), distinct from market threading. P3 is honest: latent, trivial fix, no live correctness impact today.

confirmed.title: skill_optimizer swallows the outcomes-query failure with a log-free `pass`, then scores agents against an empty outcomes dict
confirmed.claim: The per-agent signal-quality heuristic depends on the outcomes table, but the BQ query fetching it is wrapped in `except Exception: pass` with no logging — on any failure (auth, schema drift, table absence) the loop proceeds with `outcomes = {}` and computes agent scores from zero outcome data, silently degrading the skill-optimization loop. The immediately preceding failure path in the same function (line 174-176) DOES log a warning and bail, so this is an internal-consistency lapse, not a style choice.
confirmed.basis: Charter exception-discipline class; research_brief_75.0.md T8 silent-failure methodology (findings must not fail silently) and the phase-69-proven silent-degradation defect class in this repo (register.md rows on swallowed telemetry writes, e.g. orchestrator.py:1835).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Log a warning with the exception and either return [] (matching the sibling path) or explicitly mark the run degraded so downstream consumers know scores were computed without outcome data. Also add `.result(timeout=30)` while touching the line (repo BQ convention).
confirmed.verify_reason: Evidence verified at skill_optimizer.py:179-189: the outcomes BQ query is wrapped in the file's only non-binding `except Exception:` with a log-free `pass` (every other handler in the file captures and logs `e`), while the sibling reports-query failure path at 174-176 logs a warning and bails. On failure the scoring loop skips all reports and returns uniform accuracy=0.5/sample_size=0 rankings indistinguishable from a healthy empty-outcomes run — no guard or caller neutralizes the silence. Not a duplicate: the phase-69 register's skill_optimizer item is the outcome_tracker datetime TypeError, a different defect; confirmed_findings.json has no entry for this line. P3 is honest — the loop has never fired in production and outcome_tracking was historically empty, so this is an observability/debugging hazard on a dormant path, worth the one-line fix.

confirmed.title: Dead parameters whose docstrings promise behavior that does not exist (4 modules)
confirmed.claim: AST scan (params never referenced in body, decorator/interface-filtered) surfaced dead parameters whose documentation actively misleads: (a) risk_engine.compute_position_size's `symbol` docstring claims it is 'logged on errors' but symbol is never referenced, so ValueError messages lack the promised symbol context; (b) nlp_sentiment.get_nlp_sentiment forces callers to pass a positional `project_id` (+`location`) documented as 'GCP project ID for Vertex AI' although the phase-11.4 genai shim ignores both; (c) openclaw_client._headers accepts `agent_id` (and is passed it at :114) but never uses it; (d) backtest_engine._build_meta_features/_build_meta_features_batch carry unused `feature_names`/`primary_model`.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/markets/risk_engine.py:71 `symbol: str,` and :82-83 docstring `symbol: ticker / contract identifier (informational; not used
                in the formula itself but logged on errors)` — no other `symbol` reference exists in the function (grep: only :71 and :82). backend/tools/nlp_sentiment.py:60-61 `project_id: str,
    location: str = "us-central1",` with :69-70 `project_id: GCP project ID for Vertex AI
        location: GCP region`; body uses `client = get_genai_client()` only. backend/agents/openclaw_client.py:55-60 `def _headers(
    agent_id: str = "main", ...` (agent_id unused in body) and :114 `headers=_headers(agent_id, resolved_override, session_key),`. backend/backtest/backtest_engine.py:1031-1052 `_build_meta_features(..., feature_names: list[str])` / `_build_meta_features_batch(..., feature_names, primary_model)` — neither referenced in the bodies.
confirmed.basis: Charter dead-parameters + copy-paste-divergence classes; research_brief_75.0.md T8 rule 2 (verbatim evidence, AST/symbolic pre-analysis) and T6 unambiguous-parameter discipline (source #7, anthropic.com/engineering/writing-tools-for-agents). Distinct from the excluded phase-69 quant_optimizer.py:550 dead-params row and from docstring-HONEST retained params (earnings_tone/patent_tracker `api_key`, signals_server `neutral_band_pct`), which were reviewed and deliberately not reported.
confirmed.suggested_fix: risk_engine: actually include symbol in the raise messages (matching the docstring) or delete the claim. nlp_sentiment: drop project_id/location from the signature (or default them and mark deprecated) and fix the docstring. openclaw_client: remove agent_id from _headers and its call site. backtest_engine: remove the two unused meta-feature params.
confirmed.verify_reason: All four sub-claims verified in-source: (a) risk_engine.py compute_position_size takes `symbol` (:71) whose docstring (:82-83) promises it is "logged on errors", yet none of the function's ValueError raises (:97, :101, :125) nor _base_notional's (:64) include it — grep shows symbol only at :71/:82; (b) nlp_sentiment.py get_nlp_sentiment requires positional project_id (:60) + location (:61) documented as Vertex AI config (:69-70) but the body only calls the zero-arg get_genai_client() shim (:89), while callers (signals.py:95/:182, orchestrator.py:1206) uselessly thread settings.gcp_project_id/gcp_location through; (c) openclaw_client.py _headers (:55-59) never uses agent_id in its body (:61-69) yet is passed it at :114; (d) backtest_engine.py _build_meta_features (:1031-1044) never uses feature_names and _build_meta_features_batch (:1046-1065) never uses feature_names or primary_model. Not a duplicate: phase-69 register's only dead-param row is the excluded quant_optimizer vol_barrier_multiplier dead-keys item; these four modules absent from confirmed_findings.json. No wrapper/guard neutralizes any claim. Severity P3 honest: no runtime misbehavior, purely misleading docs + dead plumbing. Fixer note: nlp_sentiment callers pass the params positionally, so signature changes must update signals.py:95/:182 and orchestrator.py:1206.

confirmed.title: Deprecated `asyncio.get_event_loop()` idiom at 10 coroutine call sites (works today, brittle under the 3.14 asyncio churn)
confirmed.claim: multi_agent_orchestrator.py calls `asyncio.get_event_loop()` inside `async def` bodies at 8 sites (430, 566, 586, 690, 723, 738, 862, 1065) and task_bus.delegate does the same at :140 to create response futures. Inside a running coroutine these currently return the running loop, but the function is the deprecated pre-3.10 idiom whose no-loop behavior just changed under the project's own runtime (3.14 RuntimeError, see py-core-01); any refactor that moves these lines into sync helpers inherits the crash. `asyncio.get_running_loop()` is the documented replacement and is guaranteed-correct in exactly these contexts.
confirmed.evidence: backend/agents/multi_agent_orchestrator.py:430 `loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()` (inside async `_execute_full_flow`; same pattern at 566, 586, 690, 723, 738, 862, 1065, each immediately followed by `await loop.run_in_executor(...)`). backend/agents/task_bus.py:140 `response_fut: asyncio.Future = asyncio.get_event_loop().create_future()` inside `async def delegate`.
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T2 / source #8 (docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.14.html — asyncio policy/loop-API deprecation class called out as the 3.14 sweep target); asyncio docs designate get_running_loop() as the correct call from coroutines.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Mechanical: replace all 9 in-coroutine occurrences with `asyncio.get_running_loop()` (for task_bus, `asyncio.get_running_loop().create_future()`). Zero behavior change today; removes the deprecated-idiom trap.
confirmed.verify_reason: All 9 cited sites verified verbatim: multi_agent_orchestrator.py 430/566/586/690/723/738/862/1065 each call asyncio.get_event_loop() inside async defs (confirmed enclosing async functions for every site), and task_bus.py:140 does get_event_loop().create_future() inside async def delegate (line 123). Project venv is Python 3.14.4, so the 3.14 no-running-loop RuntimeError premise applies to the project's own runtime; no live failure today since a loop is always running at these call sites, matching the finding's P3 framing. No guard/wrapper neutralizes it, fix is mechanical (get_running_loop()), and grep of phase 69-73 registers (confirmed_findings.json, harness_proposals.json, design_audit_burndown_69.md, design_pack_73) shows no prior get_event_loop item — not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: Implicit-Optional annotation lies (`str = None`, `dict = None`) in tickets_db and spot_checks dataclasses
confirmed.claim: tickets_db.py declares 11 parameters annotated as plain `str`/`dict` with `= None` defaults (constructor :69, create_ticket :161-168, update :289-291, init_tickets_db :518), and spot_checks.py declares dataclass fields `timestamp: str = None` (:33, :52) that __post_init__ then None-checks. These are PEP 484 implicit-Optional violations: every modern type checker (mypy default no_implicit_optional since 0.990, pyright) rejects them, and the annotations misdocument the real contract (None is a legal and expected value). Runtime-safe today, but they make the modules un-typecheckable and mask genuine None-flow review.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/db/tickets_db.py:69 `def __init__(self, db_path: str = None):`; :161-168 `sender_name: str = None,
        channel_id: str = None,
        ...
        metadata: dict = None`; :518 `def init_tickets_db(db_path: str = None) -> TicketsDB:`. backend/backtest/spot_checks.py:33 `timestamp: str = None` with :35-37 `def __post_init__(self):
        if self.timestamp is None:
            self.timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()` (same pattern at :52-56).
confirmed.basis: Charter typing-correctness class; PEP 484 ('no implicit Optional') via research_brief_75.0.md T2 modern-Python idioms umbrella (source #8); the rest of the scope already uses the correct `X | None` style (e.g. models/, news/sources/), so this is divergence from the codebase's own convention.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Annotate `str | None = None` / `dict | None = None` at the 13 sites (spot_checks fields become `timestamp: str | None = None`). Purely mechanical; no runtime change.
confirmed.verify_reason: All quoted evidence verified verbatim: tickets_db.py:69 `db_path: str = None`, :161-168 (6 implicit-Optional params incl. `metadata: dict = None`), :289-291 (3 more), :518 `init_tickets_db(db_path: str = None)` = 11 sites; spot_checks.py:33/:52 `timestamp: str = None` with __post_init__ None-backfill, plus :49-51 `SpotCheckResult = None` x3 and :419 `output_dir: str = None` (finding slightly undercounts: 16 sites, not 13). None is a legal expected value at every site (guards/`or {}`/backfill), so annotations misdocument the contract; PEP 484 implicit-Optional rejected by default mypy>=0.990 and pyright. Divergence claim confirmed: backend/models/timesfm_client.py and chronos_client.py use `str | None = None`. Not a duplicate: register search (confirmed_findings.json, harness_proposals.json, phase 69-73 briefs) shows no typing/implicit-Optional item; prior tickets_db/spot_checks mentions are unrelated (SQLite scope; evaluator_agent stub). Mitigating context: no mypy/pyright config exists anywhere in the repo, so nothing is failing today — impact is prospective, keeping severity at P3 polish. Fix is purely mechanical with zero runtime change.

confirmed.title: Circular layering: services layer imports API layer (private helper) at 3 sites; api.paper_trading imports back at module level
confirmed.claim: The core trading loop (services layer) reaches UP into the FastAPI router layer twice to borrow the private `_fetch_ticker_meta` helper, and services/cron_control.py reaches up into api/cron_dashboard_api for the scheduler registry, while backend/api/paper_trading.py:21 imports services.autonomous_loop at module level -- a live api<->services cycle held together only by function-scope deferred imports. Any router split/rename of paper_trading.py breaks sector enrichment at CYCLE time (not startup), where the broad except degrades it to a warning and the sector cap silently becomes a no-op; the API layer also owns the APScheduler lifecycle (paper_trading.py:37 `_scheduler = None`, :1289 `init_scheduler`), so headless/script consumers of the loop drag the whole router graph in.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/api/paper_trading.py:21: `from backend.services.autonomous_loop import run_daily_cycle, get_loop_status`
backend/services/autonomous_loop.py:881-885:
"# latency. Without this enrichment, decide_trades sees `sector=None` on
# every candidate and the new sector cap is a no-op.
if candidates:
    try:
        from backend.api.paper_trading import _fetch_ticker_meta"
backend/services/autonomous_loop.py:899-900: "except Exception as e:
    logger.warning(\"Ticker meta enrichment failed (non-fatal): %s\", e)" (same pattern at :1375)
backend/services/cron_control.py:72-73: "# Lazy import to avoid any import-time coupling with the API layer.
from backend.api.cron_dashboard_api import get_registered_schedulers"
_fetch_ticker_meta is defined module-private at backend/api/paper_trading.py:1058.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md role charter A9 (module boundaries) + T8 methodology rule 2 (verbatim evidence). The cron_control comment itself admits the coupling ('Lazy import to avoid any import-time coupling with the API layer'). Not covered by phase-70 confirmed_findings.json (its sector-cap items are risk_overrides/frontend; grep for _fetch_ticker_meta = zero hits).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Move `_fetch_ticker_meta` into a service module (e.g. backend/services/ticker_meta.py) imported by both api/paper_trading.py and the loop; move the `_RUNNING_SCHEDULERS` registry out of api/cron_dashboard_api.py into a service (cron_control already owns the domain); keep the api->services direction as the only allowed edge. Add a cheap import-linter rule (grep in CI) forbidding `backend.api` imports under backend/services|agents|db.
confirmed.verify_reason: All quoted evidence verified verbatim: services/autonomous_loop.py imports the module-private backend.api.paper_trading._fetch_ticker_meta at function scope at lines 885 and 1375 (defined private at paper_trading.py:1058), each wrapped in a broad except that degrades failure to a warning (:899-900); paper_trading.py:21 imports services.autonomous_loop at module level, making a live api<->services cycle held apart only by deferred imports; cron_control.py:72-73 lazy-imports api/cron_dashboard_api.get_registered_schedulers with the self-admitting comment, and cron_dashboard_api.py:40 acknowledges the mutual dependency ('cron_control lazy-imports this module... so no import cycle here'). The API layer owns the scheduler lifecycle (paper_trading.py:37, :1289) as claimed. Nothing neutralizes it in context: a rename/split of paper_trading.py fails at CYCLE time inside the fail-open except, silently degrading sector enrichment (nuance: sectors collapse to the 'Unknown' bucket rather than a strict no-op, which slightly changes but does not weaken the consequence). No CLAUDE.md rule sanctions services->api imports; not in phase-69 registers or confirmed_findings.json (whose _fetch_ticker_meta hits are all sector-cap 'Unknown' findings, a different defect). P2 is the honest severity: real structural debt with a latent refactor-time failure mode, no live correctness impact today.

confirmed.title: run_daily_cycle is a single ~1546-line async function (lines 252-1797) -- the god-function inside the 3142-line god-module, with a proven defect record
confirmed.claim: The entire daily money path (screen -> analyze -> re-evaluate -> decide -> execute -> MTM -> snapshot -> learn) lives in ONE function spanning lines 252-1797 (next top-level symbol is `class SynthesisDegradedError` at :1798), i.e. ~49% of a 3142-line module, with one shared local-variable scope and one try/except envelope. This structure has already produced production defects of exactly the class the shape predicts: phase-69 register CONFIRMED that the ma_preannounce aggregator at :459 reads locals first assigned ~170 lines later (UnboundLocalError swallowed non-fatally, signal permanently dead), and queued step 72.2.3 exists because Step-8 snapshotting sits inside the same terminal try as everything else. backend/services/autonomous_loop.py:252: `async def run_daily_cycle(settings: Optional[Settings] = None, dry_run: bool = False) -> dict:`
backend/services/autonomous_loop.py:1798: `class SynthesisDegradedError(RuntimeError):`

The single-envelope claim holds in context: one try at :411 wraps the entire money path to except TimeoutError :1639 / except Exception :1644 / finally :1662 (only two small preamble tries for lock/init). confirmed.title: LLM hard budget-block and cost API depend on a private function inside a slack_bot cron-job module, and the guard fails OPEN on import failure
confirmed.claim: The canonical daily/monthly spend fetch lives as `_default_fetch_spend` (a module-private) inside backend/slack_bot/jobs/cost_budget_watcher.py, and both the core LLM client's BudgetBreachError hard-block and /api/cost-budget import it from there -- core infra (agents) and the API depending on a sibling application's job module. Because the guard's except is deliberately fail-open, any slack_bot refactor/rename/packaging change silently DISABLES budget enforcement for all trading LLM calls (one WARNING line, trading continues unbudgeted). Same inverted direction: multi_agent_orchestrator and the signals MCP server import slack_bot internals.

backend/agents/llm_client.py:426-427: "from backend.config.settings import get_settings
from backend.slack_bot.jobs.cost_budget_watcher import _default_fetch_spend"
backend/agents/llm_client.py:431-433: "except Exception as exc:
    # Fail-open: never let a broken budget API halt trading.
    logger.warning(\"cost_budget hard-block fail-open: %r\", exc)"
backend/api/cost_budget_api.py:24: `from backend.slack_bot.jobs.cost_budget_watcher import _default_fetch_spend`
backend/agents/multi_agent_orchestrator.py:1080: `from backend.slack_bot.direct_responder import get_direct_response`
backend/agents/mcp_servers/signals_server.py:465: `from backend.slack_bot.formatters import format_signal_alert  # noqa: PLC0415`
def site: backend/slack_bot/jobs/cost_budget_watcher.py:82: `def _default_fetch_spend() -> tuple[float, float]:`
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md A9 charter (missing seams) + internal-recon layer map (slack_bot is a separate process/app: 'python -m backend.slack_bot.app'). The fail-open consequence makes this latent-silent-failure class, not style.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Promote the spend fetch to backend/services/observability (it already owns api_call_log/llm cost logging) as a public `fetch_spend()`; slack_bot job, llm_client, and cost_budget_api all import from there. Keep fail-open semantics but add a counter/alert when the guard degrades (observability alerting seam already exists).
confirmed.verify_reason: All quoted evidence verified verbatim: llm_client.py:427 imports _default_fetch_spend (a private explicitly excluded from cost_budget_watcher.py:118's __all__) from the slack_bot cron-job module, inside a try whose except (lines 432-437) logs one WARNING and caches fail_open — so any slack_bot rename/refactor/packaging change silently disables the budget hard-block. cost_budget_api.py:24/126, multi_agent_orchestrator.py:1080, and signals_server.py:465 confirm the repeated agents/API->slack_bot inverted dependency. Compounding: _default_fetch_spend itself fails open to (0.0, 0.0) on any exception. NOT a duplicate: the phase-69 register item at llm_client.py:431 (audit_phase69/register.md line 54) claims a different defect (meter counts BQ bytes-billed, not LLM dollars) and merely quotes the import as evidence; no 69.x-73.x step queued a fix (git log shows llm_client budget code untouched). P2 stands: fail-open is deliberate/documented with an env escape hatch, and the guard's current protective value is already dubious per phase-69, so this is architecture debt with a latent silent-degradation consequence, not a live P0/P1 break. Fix should be coordinated with the phase-69 wrong-metric item since both point at relocating the spend fetch into backend/services/observability.

confirmed.title: BQ dataset-resolution logic copy-pasted 14x with per-copy hardcoded fallback; readers and writers of the same table resolve the dataset differently
confirmed.claim: `def _resolve_target(project, dataset)` is verbatim-duplicated in 14 modules (intel/source_registry.py:45, intel/novelty_client.py:128, intel/prompt_patch_queue.py:50, news/bq_writer.py:41, alt_data/{congress:202, reddit_wsb:129, finra_short:191, google_trends:89, http:131, f13:340, etf_flows:95, twitter:102, features:60, hiring:122}), each embedding its own `"pyfinagent_data"` literal fallback, and ~25 modules construct raw `bigquery.Client()` instead of using the shared backend/db/bigquery_client.py seam. Meanwhile consumers hardcode the dataset outright: pead_signal queries `pyfinagent_data.calendar_events` as a literal while the writer resolves via settings.bq_dataset_observability -- so changing that setting splits writer and reader across datasets silently, and dataset policy changes require 14+ synchronized edits.

confirmed.suggested_fix: Extract ONE `resolve_bq_target(project=None, dataset=None)` (backend/db/bq_target.py) and a shared client factory; mechanical 14-site replacement with byte-identical behavior; fix pead_signal.py:336 to use the resolver. confirmed.verify_reason: All evidence verified byte-for-byte: 14 verbatim `_resolve_target` copies at the exact claimed file:line locations, each embedding the "pyfinagent_data" literal 3x (source_registry.py:57, http.py:142, bq_writer.py:53, f13.py:351 spot-checked identical). No shared resolver exists in backend/db/ (only bigquery_client.py, tickets_db.py). Reader/writer split confirmed: pead_signal.py:336 hardcodes `pyfinagent_data.calendar_events` while settings.py:153 bq_dataset_observability (whose description explicitly covers calendar_events) governs the writer path — changing the setting silently splits them. 56 raw bigquery.Client( sites confirm the client-seam bypass. confirmed.title: Kill-switch cap defaults defined independently in 3 places; MCP risk tool evaluates operator-tunable caps from its own constants and reuses the max-DD constant as the trailing-DD default
confirmed.claim: The 4%/10% kill-switch caps exist as (1) settings.py paper_daily_loss_limit_pct/paper_trailing_dd_limit_pct Fields, (2) an independent copy in api/settings_api.py's response model defaults, and (3) independent DEFAULT_* constants in the MAS risk MCP server whose kill_switch tool signature defaults `trailing_dd_limit_pct: float = DEFAULT_MAX_DD_CAP_PCT` -- reusing a semantically different constant (projected-DD veto cap) as the trailing-DD limit because both happen to be 10.0. An operator who tunes the caps via Settings changes the live engine but NOT the MCP tool defaults MAS agents evaluate with, and any future change to one 10.0 silently drags the other. (No threshold-value change proposed -- do-no-harm respected; PBO threshold census is queued 73.4.2 and untouched here.)
confirmed.evidence:
backend/config/settings.py:528-529: `paper_daily_loss_limit_pct: float = Field(4.0, ...)` / `paper_trailing_dd_limit_pct: float = Field(10.0, ...)`
backend/api/settings_api.py:111-112: `paper_daily_loss_limit_pct: float = 4.0` / `paper_trailing_dd_limit_pct: float = 10.0`
backend/agents/mcp_servers/risk_server.py:27-30: "# Default hard caps (can be overridden via settings in future step).
DEFAULT_PBO_VETO_THRESHOLD = 0.5
DEFAULT_MAX_DD_CAP_PCT = 10.0
DEFAULT_DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT_PCT = 4.0"
backend/agents/mcp_servers/risk_server.py:66-67: "daily_loss_limit_pct: float = DEFAULT_DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT_PCT,
trailing_dd_limit_pct: float = DEFAULT_MAX_DD_CAP_PCT) -> dict:"
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md A9 charter (config sprawl) -- the risk_server comment itself defers the settings wiring ('can be overridden via settings in future step'). P3 (not P2) because the MCP tool is advisory to MAS agents, not the live engine's enforcement path, and values currently agree.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Have the kill_switch tool resolve defaults from get_settings() at call time (keeping explicit args as overrides); introduce DEFAULT_TRAILING_DD_LIMIT_PCT so the trailing default no longer aliases the max-DD-cap constant; derive settings_api response defaults from the Settings model instead of re-typing them.
confirmed.verify_reason: All three independent copies verified: settings.py:528-529 Fields, settings_api.py:111-112 response-model defaults (dead-but-duplicated — response is actually built from get_settings() at :377-378 via getattr with re-typed 4.0/10.0 fallbacks, a fourth micro-copy), and risk_server.py:27-30 DEFAULT_* constants. The aliasing is real and semantically wrong: DEFAULT_MAX_DD_CAP_PCT serves as the projected-DD veto cap in evaluate_candidate (:165,:206) AND as the trailing-DD default in kill_switch (:67) only because both are 10.0. No neutralizer: the live engine passes settings values (paper_trader.py:1094-1108, paper_trading.py:500-559) but the MCP tool never consults get_settings(), so operator tuning diverges engine vs MAS-advisory tool. Not queued: confirmed_findings.json has no match; 73.4/73.4.2 census covers PBO thresholds only, which this finding excludes. The line-27 comment defers settings wiring but no register item tracks it; in-scope per A9 config-sprawl charter. P3 honest: advisory path only, values currently agree.

confirmed.title: Module name collision: backend/autonomous_loop.py (dormant phase-3.3 planner loop) vs backend/services/autonomous_loop.py (live money path) -- two different systems, one leaf name
confirmed.claim: Two unrelated modules share the name `autonomous_loop.py`: the 619-line root module (phase-3.3 Planner->Generator->Evaluator strategy loop, imported only by one integration test and the manual scripts/harness/run_autonomous_loop.py) and the 3142-line services module (the live daily trading cycle). backend/services/autonomous_loop.py (wc -l: 3142)

confirmed.verify_reason: All evidence verified: backend/autonomous_loop.py:2 header and 619/3142 line counts exact; importers confirmed (run_autonomous_loop.py:27 plus 2 test files, minor drift from claimed "+1 test"); harness_proposals.json evaluator quote verbatim. Both import paths bind different real modules with no guard. No prior register item addresses the collision/rename; harness_proposals owns only the dormant module's evaluator hygiene, correctly excluded. Caveat on the fix: bundling backend/autonomous_harness.py must also update tests/verify_phase_23_8_3.py, which asserts that module's header content and importability, so the harness leg touches 3 files, not 1 — but the live services/autonomous_loop.py path is untouched either way. confirmed.title: scripts/ bootstraps imports via 100 ad-hoc sys.path mutations in three inconsistent idioms -- no installed-package seam
confirmed.claim: With no pyproject.toml / editable install, every script hand-rolls repo-root path injection: 100 sys.path.insert/append occurrences across scripts/ in at least three idioms (os.path.join+dirname, Path.parents[1], Path.parent.parent) -- and scripts/harness/run_autonomous_loop.py:25 inserts its OWN directory (scripts/harness), a different target again. Scripts silently depend on invocation cwd/layout; moving a script one directory deep breaks its bootstrap; and the duplicated preamble is exactly the class of copy-paste the ASCII-sweep maintenance keeps paying for.
confirmed.evidence:
grep -rn 'sys.path' scripts/ --include='*.py' -> 100 hits, e.g.:
scripts/cleanup_phase_23_1_15.py:51: `sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), ".."))`
scripts/diag_all_strategies.py:13: `sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]))`
scripts/smoketest_stages_5_through_13.py:16: `sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))`
scripts/harness/run_autonomous_loop.py:25: `sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))`
research_brief_75.0.md internal recon: "No `pyproject.toml`; deps in `backend/requirements.txt` (59 lines)."
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T2 (packaging: manifest+lock best practice presumes an installable project; anchor #11 https://docs.astral.sh/uv/pip/compile/) + A9 charter (scripts/ dependency structure). Lockfile/pinning itself is A8's headline -- this finding is the import-path seam only.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add a minimal pyproject.toml ([project] + setuptools backend, packages=["backend"]) and `pip install -e .` into the venv; then a mechanical sweep deleting the sys.path preambles (scripts import `backend.*` directly). Pairs naturally with A8's uv lockfile remediation.
confirmed.verify_reason: All quoted evidence verified verbatim at the cited lines; the finding's "100 sys.path hits" is an undercount (grep returns 141). No pyproject.toml/setup.py/setup.cfg exists at repo root, so no installed-package seam. The three inconsistent idioms are confirmed, and scripts/harness/run_autonomous_loop.py:25 is worse than claimed: its comment says "Add project root to path" but it inserts scripts/harness/, then imports backend.autonomous_loop (which lives only at repo root) — so its bootstrap is a no-op and the script only works when invoked from the repo root with cwd implicitly on sys.path, directly demonstrating the cwd-dependence claim. Not queued in MASTERPLAN.md, confirmed_findings.json, harness_proposals.json, or the phase-69 audit register; not sanctioned by CLAUDE.md. P3 is honest: no live defect, everything works when invoked canonically, but the duplicated preamble is real maintenance debt worth the suggested pyproject + editable-install fix.

confirmed.title: /agents MAS observability page structurally cannot authenticate: SSE opened with withCredentials:false and raw credential-less fetches against auth-required /api/mas
confirmed.claim: The /agents Glass-Box page can never receive live MAS data from an authenticated backend: the shared SSE hook explicitly disables cookies (EventSource cannot send Authorization headers at all), and the page's stats/dashboard fetches bypass api.ts with no credentials option, so all three transports get 401 from the backend auth middleware (cross-origin :3000->:8000 sends no cookies by default; /api/mas is not in _PUBLIC_PATHS). The page permanently shows 'Lost connection to MAS event stream after 5 failures. Backend may be down.' and empty stats/OpenClaw panels.
confirmed.evidence:
useEventSource.ts:91: `const es = new EventSource(url, { withCredentials: false });`
agents/page.tsx:221: `const res = await fetch(\`${API_BASE}/api/mas/events/stats\`);`
agents/page.tsx:229: `const res = await fetch(\`${API_BASE}/api/mas/dashboard\`);`
backend/api/mas_events.py:19: `router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/mas", tags=["MAS Observability"])`
backend/main.py:406 `_PUBLIC_PATHS = (` lists /api/health, /api/changelog, /api/auth, /api/cost-budget, /api/jobs/status, /api/harness/*, /api/signals, /api/observability, /api/sovereign, /docs, /openapi.json, /redoc — no /api/mas
backend/api/auth.py:193: `if not candidates:` -> `raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Authentication required")`
api.ts:87 (the canonical client these bypass): `credentials: "include",`
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T5/API2 ('Bearer-token check on FastAPI + NextAuth JWE decrypt path — backend/api/auth.py::get_current_user wired in main.py; audit every router actually mounts it') + .claude/rules/frontend.md:15 ('API client: src/lib/api.ts — Bearer token auth ... on all requests'). Caveat disclosed: reading backend/.env was permission-denied, so I cannot confirm whether DEV_LOCALHOST_BYPASS=1 masks this locally; auth.py:150-153 shows even that bypass requires client host 127.0.0.1/::1, so Tailscale (100.x) access fails regardless.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Open the EventSource with { withCredentials: true } (cookie auth is the only option for SSE; backend CORS already sets allow_credentials=true and get_current_user already accepts the authjs session cookie), and route the stats/dashboard fetches through apiFetch (or add credentials:'include'). Verify against the running authed backend per frontend.md live-UI protocol.
confirmed.verify_reason: Mechanism fully verified: useEventSource.ts:91 opens SSE with withCredentials:false and agents/page.tsx:221/229 use raw credential-less fetch (fetch default is same-origin mode; API_BASE is cross-origin http://localhost:8000), the SSE URL carries no token, /api/mas is not in main.py _PUBLIC_PATHS so auth_and_security_middleware calls get_current_user on every request, and auth.py:193 raises 401 when neither Bearer header nor authjs.session-token cookie arrives — auth.py's own comment says cookie auth only works because api.ts uses credentials:'include', which these three transports bypass. One correction to the claim's absolutism: phase-69 register evidence (confirmed_findings.json) shows the live launchd plist sets DEV_LOCALHOST_BYPASS=1 (confirmed via ps eww on the running uvicorn), so same-machine localhost browsing works today via the 127.0.0.1 bypass at auth.py:150-153. But the defect is real and latent-serious: on the explicitly supported Tailscale path (CORS regex allows http://100.x origins) client.host is the 100.x peer, the bypass never fires, and all three transports 401 → permanent 'Lost connection' + empty stats panels; same for any deployment without the dev bypass. Suggested fix is correct (backend accepts session cookie, allow_credentials=True). No duplicate in phase-69/70/71/72/73 registers. P1 stands (not P0: operator's localhost path currently works via the bypass).

confirmed.title: 401 handler hard-redirects to /login with no already-on-login guard while the root-mounted LivePortfolioProvider polls authed endpoints on /login — reload loop on the login page
confirmed.claim: Root layout mounts LivePortfolioProvider on EVERY route including /login (middleware.ts:15 exempts /login, so it renders logged-out). On mount the provider immediately fires 3 authed calls (getPaperTradingStatus/getPaperPortfolio/getPaperSnapshots); logged-out they 401; apiFetch's 401 branch assigns window.location.href='/login' with no pathname check, which on /login triggers a full reload -> remount -> immediate re-poll -> reload loop (~sub-second cadence), interrupting Google SSO/passkey ceremonies and generating continuous 401 noise. The provider's own comment admits session gating is deferred ('so it can gate on session in a future hardening pass').
confirmed.evidence:
api.ts:112-116: `if (res.status === 401) {` / `if (typeof window !== "undefined") {` / `window.location.href = "/login";` / `}` / `throw new Error("Session expired. Redirecting to login.");`
app/layout.tsx:36: `<LivePortfolioProvider>` (root layout, wraps all routes)
app/layout.tsx:34-35 comment: `Mounted inside AuthProvider so it can gate on session in a future hardening pass.`
live-portfolio-context.tsx:142-146: `useEffect(() => { void refresh(); const id = window.setInterval(() => void refresh(), POLL_INTERVAL_MS); return () => window.clearInterval(id); }, [refresh]);`
middleware.ts:15: `pathname === "/login" ||` (excluded from auth redirect)
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T3 rule 8 (error/loading states + React 19 error-boundary alignment) and T5/API2 auth-path audit; .claude/rules/frontend.md:15 documents the '401 -> redirect to /login' convention whose unguarded implementation is the defect. Mechanism verified end-to-end in code (read-only; no live browser run).
confirmed.suggested_fix: In apiFetch's 401 branch, skip the redirect when window.location.pathname === '/login'. Additionally gate LivePortfolioProvider polling on session status (useSession) or pathname !== '/login' as its own comment already plans.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified end-to-end: apiFetch's 401 branch (api.ts:112-116) assigns window.location.href='/login' with no already-on-login guard; LivePortfolioProvider is mounted in the root layout (layout.tsx:36) which also wraps /login (app/login/ has only page.tsx, no isolating layout; middleware.ts:15 exempts /login so it renders logged out); on mount it immediately fires getPaperTradingStatus/getPaperPortfolio/getPaperSnapshots (live-portfolio-context.tsx:142-146). Backend get_current_user (backend/api/auth.py:191-192) returns 401 for requests with no Bearer token and no session cookie whenever AUTH_SECRET is set, i.e. the logged-out production config. Nothing neutralizes it: AuthProvider is a bare SessionProvider rendering children unconditionally, and Promise.allSettled catches the throw only AFTER the redirect side-effect has fired inside apiFetch. Assigning location.href to the current URL triggers a full reload -> remount -> re-poll -> 401 -> reload loop, interrupting SSO/passkey on /login. Not fixed, not sanctioned by .claude/rules/frontend.md (which documents the 401->login convention but not redirecting FROM /login), and not in the phase-69 register (its LivePortfolioProvider items cover staleness/freshness/pnlToday, not this). P1 honest: live on the production auth path but a single-operator cockpit with long-lived sessions limits exposure frequency (not P0).

confirmed.title: Reports comparison price charts are silently dead: raw credential-less fetch to auth-required /api/charts always 401s and the catch ignores it
confirmed.claim: The multi-ticker compare flow fetches /api/charts/{ticker} with a bare fetch (no credentials, no Bearer) cross-origin; /api/charts is not in _PUBLIC_PATHS so the backend returns 401; `res.ok` is false and the catch comment says 'ignore chart failures', so priceData stays empty and the normalized price-comparison chart renders empty on every compare, with no error surfaced — a silently dead feature.
confirmed.evidence:
reports/page.tsx:197-200: `const res = await fetch(\`${API_BASE}/api/charts/${encodeURIComponent(t)}?period=1y\`);` / `if (res.ok) prices[t] = await res.json();` / `} catch { /* ignore chart failures */ }`
backend/api/charts.py:13: `router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/charts", tags=["charts"])` (absent from _PUBLIC_PATHS at backend/main.py:406)
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T5/API2 auth-path audit + .claude/rules/frontend.md:56 error-states rule ('Never use .catch(() => null) on ALL calls in a group ... surface an error banner') and frontend-layout.md §8 'Never show blank space'.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Route the chart fetch through apiFetch (adds Bearer/cookie + 30s abort) and surface a partial-failure notice when price series are unavailable instead of silently omitting the chart.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified: reports/page.tsx:198 uses a bare fetch (no Bearer, default same-origin credentials) against a cross-origin API_BASE with no Next.js rewrite proxy, so neither header token nor NextAuth cookie reaches the backend; /api/charts is absent from _PUBLIC_PATHS (backend/main.py:406-423) so auth middleware 401s (auth.py:135-189 requires Bearer or credentials:"include" cookie, which apiFetch at api.ts:65-87 provides but this call bypasses); res.ok is false and the catch swallows everything, and line 447 conditionally hides the Price Performance section when normalizedChart is empty — so the compare price chart silently never appears (minor drift from "renders empty": it is omitted entirely, same net defect). Only dev-only bypass flags (DEV_LOCALHOST_BYPASS, documented never-in-prod) could mask it. No duplicate in confirmed_findings.json, research_brief_75.0.md, or phase-69 burndown. StockChart.tsx:94 shares the same bare-fetch defect, confirming this is an unguarded pattern. P2 is honest: silently dead user-facing visualization, no correctness/security/financial impact.

confirmed.title: featureFlags env-var override is dead code in the client bundle (dynamic process.env lookup is never inlined) and render-time flag reads risk hydration mismatch
confirmed.claim: Next.js statically inlines only literal `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_*` member expressions at build time; the computed lookup `envKey in process.env` / `process.env[envKey]` is never replaced, so in the browser the documented reading order 'NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_<KEY>=true env var > localStorage > default' silently does not work — an operator setting NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_COCKPIT_V2=true gets no client effect. Worse, during SSR (Node runtime) the dynamic lookup DOES work, so an env-flagged feature renders server-side then flips off on hydration (mismatch). Separately, isFeatureEnabled reads localStorage during render in CommandPalette, so any localStorage override diverges from the server HTML (React 19 hydration error).
confirmed.evidence:
featureFlags.ts:7 doc: `Reading order: NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_<KEY>=true env var > localStorage > default.`
featureFlags.ts:40-42: `const envKey = \`NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_${key.toUpperCase()}\`;` / `if (typeof process !== "undefined" && process.env && envKey in process.env) {` / `const raw = process.env[envKey];`
CommandPalette.tsx:72: `const enabled = isFeatureEnabled("command_palette");` (called during render)
CommandPalette.tsx:83: `if (!enabled) return null;`
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md source #5 (Next.js server-and-client-components, read in full): 'only environment variables prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ are included in the client bundle ... otherwise replaced with an empty string' — inlining is static text replacement, so dynamic access is the documented bug class (T3 rule 2: 'non-prefixed env reads inside client components silently become ""').
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the dynamic lookup with a static literal map (`const ENV_OVERRIDES = { command_palette: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_COMMAND_PALETTE, ... }`) so each expression is inlined, and move localStorage reads out of render (useSyncExternalStore or a mounted-effect state) to keep SSR/CSR consistent.
confirmed.verify_reason: Confirmed by the compiled artifacts: client chunk .next/static/chunks/app/layout.js retains the dynamic `envKey in process.env` lookup unreplaced (while the adjacent `typeof window` check in readLocalStorage WAS statically inlined to false, proving only static expressions get inlined), so the documented env-override tier is dead in the browser; the server bundle keeps a working dynamic lookup, creating the SSR/CSR split. Not a duplicate (no featureFlags item in handoff registers), not neutralized (CommandPalette is statically mounted in layout.tsx, no ssr:false/mounted guard). Minor overstatement: for the sole current caller the hydration error is theoretical (disabled `return null` vs enabled closed Command.Dialog both emit no DOM), and the docstring semi-acknowledges the localStorage overlay — so this stays P2 debt, not P1: no env flag is set in .env.local and no content-gating flag consumer exists yet, but the operator env control silently does not work and will bite when cockpit_v2 etc. gate real markup.

confirmed.title: OpsStatusBar: all four fetchers individually .catch(() => null) making the outer catch and failRef unreachable — backend death freezes Gate/Kill/Cycle segments as stale-but-live with no error, forever
confirmed.claim: Every call inside the Promise.all carries its own .catch(() => null), so the surrounding try/catch can never fire and failRef (the consecutive-failure counter) is dead code that is never read anywhere. When the backend dies, all four results are null, no state updates, no error banner, and the 60s poll continues indefinitely — the operator status bar (including the KILL segment) keeps displaying the last-known gate/kill-switch values as if current. This violates both written rules in the very file frontend.md names as the canonical implementation.
confirmed.evidence:
OpsStatusBar.tsx:74: `const failRef = useRef(0);`
OpsStatusBar.tsx:78-83: `const [g, k, f, c] = await Promise.all([` / `getPaperGate().catch(() => null),` / `getPaperKillSwitchState().catch(() => null) as Promise<KillSwitchState | null>,` / `getPaperFreshness().catch(() => null) as Promise<Freshness | null>,` / `getPaperCyclesHistory(1).catch(() => null),` / `]);`
OpsStatusBar.tsx:88-91: `failRef.current = 0;` / `} catch {` / `failRef.current += 1;` / `}` (failRef is never read; grep confirms no consumer)
confirmed.basis: .claude/rules/frontend.md:56 ('Never use .catch(() => null) on ALL calls in a group. If all calls in a Promise.all fail, surface an error banner') and :57 ('Polling loops (setInterval) must count consecutive failures and stop after 5 with an error message'); frontend-layout.md §4.5 names OpsStatusBar.tsx the canonical operator-status implementation. Distinct from the phase-70 register items (cockpit-helpers.tsx dead kill tile; live-portfolio-context freshness aging) — different component and mechanism, deduped against handoff/current/confirmed_findings.json.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Detect the all-null outcome (g==null && k==null && f==null && c==null), increment failRef there, render a stale/error segment state after 5 consecutive failures, and stop or back off the interval; keep individual catches for partial degradation only.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in OpsStatusBar.tsx: all four fetchers in the Promise.all (lines 78-83) carry individual .catch(() => null), so the await never rejects and the outer catch (89-91) is unreachable; failRef (74) is written but never read (no >=5 check, no setError, no interval stop), so the 60s poll (97) continues forever and on backend death the if-guards (84-87) skip updates, leaving Gate/Kill/Cycle segments frozen at last-known values (KillSegment keeps showing stale ACTIVE/PAUSED + breach pcts) with no error banner. This violates both explicit rules in .claude/rules/frontend.md (all-null group must surface an error banner; polling must stop after 5 consecutive failures), and the intended pattern is proven implementable by siblings KillSwitchPanel.tsx:53, CycleHealthStrip.tsx:82, useLivePrices.ts:50 which all do failRef.current >= 5 -> setError. No OpsStatusBar item exists in confirmed_findings.json or any handoff register; the phase-70 items are different components. P2 is honest: real operator-observability defect that only manifests when the backend is dead, misleads rather than mis-executes (kill actions would fail loudly via window.alert), so not P1.

confirmed.title: Multiple polling loops violate the mandatory stop-after-5-consecutive-failures rule (poll a dead backend forever, some silently)
confirmed.claim: frontend.md makes failure-counted polling mandatory, and /cron + useEventSource implement it — but at least four other loops poll forever: agents stats/dashboard every 15s with silent catches ('// silent'); observability freshness every 30s; HarnessDashboard seed-stability every 30s with .catch(() => {}) (its Promise.all also catches every call so an all-fail renders an empty dashboard, not the error banner); AutoresearchLeaderboard every refreshMs with no counter. useLivePrices' doc comment claims 'stops polling after 5 in a row' but the code only sets an error message and keeps polling — the circuit never opens.
confirmed.evidence:
agents/page.tsx:241: `const interval = setInterval(() => { fetchStats(); fetchOpenClaw(); }, 15000);` (catches at :223-224, :235-236 are `catch { // silent }`)
observability/page.tsx:77: `const id = setInterval(() => void load(), 30_000);`
HarnessDashboard.tsx:223-225: `const interval = setInterval(() => {` / `getSeedStability().then(setSeedStability).catch(() => {});` / `}, 30_000);`
AutoresearchLeaderboard.tsx:100-102: `refresh(); const id = setInterval(refresh, refreshMs); return () => clearInterval(id);`
useLivePrices.ts:22 comment: `Consecutive failures: stops polling after 5 in a row` vs :49-52: `failRef.current += 1; if (failRef.current >= 5) { setError(...); }` — no stop.
confirmed.basis: .claude/rules/frontend.md:57: 'Polling loops (setInterval) must count consecutive failures and stop after 5 with an error message. Never poll forever on a dead backend.' (Positive control: app/cron/page.tsx:161-178 implements the rule correctly, proving the intended pattern.)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Extract the cron-page failuresRef pattern (or a small usePoller hook) and apply it to the four sites; in useLivePrices actually clear the interval (or skip ticks) once failRef reaches 5, matching its own comment.
confirmed.verify_reason: All five sites verified in-context. frontend.md:57 makes failure-counted stop-after-5 polling mandatory; cron/page.tsx:161-178 implements it (failuresRef + stoppedRef), proving intent. Violations confirmed: (1) agents/page.tsx:241 polls stats+dashboard every 15s with 'catch { // silent }' at 223-225/235-237 — no counter, no error, forever; (2) observability/page.tsx:76-77 polls every 30s forever (surfaces error but never stops); (3) HarnessDashboard.tsx:222-226 polls seed-stability every 30s with .catch(() => {}), and its mount Promise.all catches every individual call so the outer .catch(setError) is unreachable — all-fail renders an empty dashboard, additionally violating frontend.md's group-catch rule; (4) AutoresearchLeaderboard.tsx:97-102 polls every refreshMs with no counter; (5) useLivePrices (at src/lib/, not src/hooks/ — minor path drift, content exact) comments 'stops polling after 5 in a row' at lines 22-24 but code at 49-52 only sets an error; interval at :58 is never cleared and tick() has no circuit check — the comment is false. Not a duplicate: phase-69 register item live-portfolio-context.tsx:91 cites useLivePrices' keep-polling in its evidence but its defect is the freshness band not aging client-side, a different bug/fix; no prior register item targets the polling-rule violation or the other four loops. P2 honest: mandatory-rule debt with degraded UX on dead backend, not live money correctness.
