confirmed.verify_reason: Repo-wide grep over frontend (*.tsx/*.ts/*.js/*.mjs) matches only the four component files themselves — zero imports anywhere including app pages and tests. Quoted evidence verified: StockChart.tsx:94 bare cross-origin fetch (API_BASE from NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL, no credentials, no ignore-flag cleanup, deps [ticker, period]); ResearchInvestigator.tsx:33 bare POST to /api/investigate (backend/api/investigate.py exists). Context confirms genuine orphaning, not pending wiring: harness_log.md phase-15 entries show AltDataPanel and TransformerForecastPanel were once mounted in signals/page.tsx, but the current signals page contains no reference — they were dropped in a rewrite and left on disk. Phase-69 audit register (register.md, confirmed/contested/refuted JSONs) contains no entry for these components; not a duplicate of any phase-69/70/71/72/73 item. P3 is honest: dead-code debt whose embedded fetch anti-pattern is not live precisely because nothing imports it.

confirmed.title: api.ts fires an extra /api/auth/session round-trip on EVERY backend call — polling surfaces double their request volume
confirmed.claim: getAuthToken() awaits fetch('/api/auth/session') inside every apiFetch. In production the authjs session cookie is HttpOnly, so the document.cookie scan always misses and the entire session fetch exists only to choose between null and the 'session-active' sentinel (the backend reads the cookie itself). With the root LivePortfolioProvider (3 calls/60s), useLivePrices (1/60s), OpsStatusBar (4/60s), CycleHealthStrip (2/30s), Sidebar health (1/30s) etc., an open cockpit generates ~14+ redundant session requests/min and adds a serial round-trip of latency to every API call.
confirmed.evidence:
api.ts:46-49: `async function getAuthToken(): Promise<string | null> {` / `if (typeof window === "undefined") return null;` / `try {` / `const res = await fetch("/api/auth/session");`
api.ts:58-59: `if (tokenCookie) return tokenCookie.split("=").slice(1).join("=");` / `return session?.user ? "session-active" : null;`
api.ts:65-66: `async function apiFetch<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {` / `const token = await getAuthToken();`
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T3 (client-SPA architecture note, rule 1) — perf debt class; backend auth already accepts the cookie directly (backend/api/auth.py:184-189), making the per-call session probe redundant.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Memoize getAuthToken with a short TTL (e.g. 60s) or drop the session probe entirely and always send credentials:'include' + the sentinel, invalidating the cache on 401.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified: api.ts:46-63 getAuthToken awaits fetch('/api/auth/session') with zero memoization, and apiFetch (line 66) calls it on every request. Backend auth.py:182 explicitly excludes the 'session-active' sentinel from token candidates and reads the HttpOnly session cookie directly (auth.py:184-189), which apiFetch already sends via credentials:'include' (api.ts:87) — so the per-call session probe is pure overhead: the document.cookie scan can never hit an HttpOnly cookie and the resulting header contributes nothing to auth. Polling surfaces (live-portfolio-context 60s, OpsStatusBar, CycleHealthStrip 30s) all route through apiFetch, confirming the redundant-request-volume claim. No neutralizing wrapper (SessionProvider refetch does not intercept raw fetch), not fixed, not intentional per .claude/rules/frontend.md, and no duplicate in confirmed_findings.json or audit_phase69 registers. P3 is honest: local-origin request, latency/noise only, no correctness impact.

confirmed.title: React 19 deprecated <Context.Provider> form still used in the two live context providers
confirmed.claim: React 19 renders <Context> directly as the provider and announces Context.Provider 'will be deprecated in future versions'; the codebase is otherwise React-19-clean (zero forwardRef hits) but both app-level providers still use the deprecated form, accruing known-removal debt on the app's most central wiring.
confirmed.evidence:
live-portfolio-context.tsx:245-247: `<LivePortfolioContext.Provider value={value}>` / `{children}` / `</LivePortfolioContext.Provider>`
app/paper-trading/layout.tsx:478: `<PaperTradingDataContext.Provider value={ctxValue}>` and :506: `</PaperTradingDataContext.Provider>` (only remaining hits repo-wide; forwardRef: zero hits)
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md source #6 (React 19 release post, read in full): '<Context> provider' replaces <Context.Provider>, which 'will be deprecated'; T3 rule 3 makes this a checkable migration-debt item.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Mechanical rename to <LivePortfolioContext value={...}> / <PaperTradingDataContext value={...}> (React 19 minimum already satisfied by react 19.2.4 in the lockfile).
confirmed.verify_reason: Evidence verified exactly: live-portfolio-context.tsx:245-247 and paper-trading/layout.tsx:478/506 are the only two Context.Provider usages in frontend/src (forwardRef: zero hits), and package-lock.json resolves react 19.2.4, so the React-19 <Context value={...}> form is available and the .Provider form is the announced-deprecated legacy. No wrapper/guard neutralizes it, .claude/rules/frontend.md does not sanction the old form, and no phase-69/70/71/72/73 register item covers this (the only handoff mention of Context.Provider is the 75.0 research brief). Zero runtime impact today, so P3 polish is the honest severity; the fix is a mechanical two-file rename.

confirmed.title: PaperTradingStatus type omits the not_initialized variant; layout.tsx dereferences .loop.running and crashes on it
confirmed.claim: GET /api/paper-trading/status returns a bare {status:'not_initialized', message} object when no portfolio exists, but the frontend PaperTradingStatus interface declares portfolio/position_count/scheduler_active/next_run/loop as non-optional, and paper-trading/layout.tsx dereferences status?.loop.running and s.loop.running unguarded -- a TypeError crash of the whole paper-trading section on any fresh/reset install that tsc cannot see.
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T3 rule 6 (strictness must model actual payloads) and T5 API3 note ('pydantic response_model prevents overexposure -- audit raw-dict returns'): the endpoint returns an untyped raw dict with two incompatible shapes, and the TS type models only one. Latent-crash variant not covered by phase-69 register rows 72-75 (those cover max_drawdown_pct/polling/freshness, not the not_initialized union).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Model the union in types.ts (e.g. type PaperTradingStatusResponse = NotInitialized | PaperTradingStatus, or make portfolio/loop/... optional) and guard layout.tsx:217/263 with status.status !== 'not_initialized' before touching .loop; ideally give the backend endpoint a declared response_model so the two shapes are explicit.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified end-to-end. Backend (backend/api/paper_trading.py:130-131) returns a bare {"status":"not_initialized","message"} dict with no response_model; the TS type (types.ts:702-721) declares portfolio/position_count/scheduler_active/next_run/loop as required, so tsc cannot flag the mismatch. layout.tsx:217 `!!status?.loop.running` does NOT save it: status is a truthy object in the not_initialized case, so the optional chain proceeds to `undefined.running` and throws a TypeError during render. api.ts:317 does no normalization, and there is no error.tsx in app/ or app/paper-trading/, so the whole paper-trading layout and all child routes crash on any fresh/reset install. The layout even models the state (isInitialized at line 215, Initialize-Fund CTA) but that empty-state UI is unreachable because line 217 throws first. Line 263 (s.loop.running) is the same class. Not a duplicate: the only related register entry (confirmed_findings.json:477) is a refuted Settings-visibility finding covering different code. P2 is honest: guaranteed crash, but latent (only fires with no portfolio row; live fund is initialized).

confirmed.title: FullSettings drift both directions: missing paper_use_claude_code_route/paper_cycle_max_seconds; phantom thinking_budget_* fields
confirmed.claim: Frontend FullSettings omits two backend-writable operator controls (paper_use_claude_code_route, paper_cycle_max_seconds -- whose backend comment explicitly promises 'Operator can lower via Settings UI', and no Manage-tab control exists), while declaring five extended-thinking fields (enable_thinking, thinking_budget_*) that the backend FullSettings/SettingsUpdate models neither return nor accept -- any UI built on them would silently no-op.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md role charter A2 ('types.ts drift vs actual FastAPI response models -- compare field-by-field') + .claude/rules/frontend.md 'Types: src/lib/types.ts -- all TypeScript interfaces for backend responses'. Pydantic ignores unknown fields by default, so phantom writes fail silently (T5 API3 mass-assignment/property-level audit class).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add paper_use_claude_code_route + paper_cycle_max_seconds to frontend FullSettings (and surface them on the Manage tab per the backend comment); delete the five thinking fields or wire them into backend FullSettings/SettingsUpdate if the feature is still intended.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified both directions of the drift. (a) Backend FullSettings has paper_use_claude_code_route (settings_api.py:118) and paper_cycle_max_seconds (:123), both writable via SettingsUpdate (:169,171), persisted via _FIELD_TO_ENV (:307-308), and returned via _settings_to_full (:381,383) — a complete operator-control write path whose comment explicitly promises 'Operator can lower via Settings UI' — yet frontend FullSettings (types.ts:530-583) omits both, updateSettings is typed Partial<FullSettings> (api.ts:251) so no typed write is expressible, and grep shows zero occurrences of either name anywhere in frontend/src (no Manage-tab control). (b) types.ts:546-550 declares enable_thinking + four thinking_budget_* fields that appear nowhere else in frontend/src and nowhere in backend FullSettings/SettingsUpdate (settings_api.py:61-174); backend never returns them and Pydantic default extra-handling would silently drop writes, so any UI built on them no-ops. Not fixed, not blessed by .claude/rules/frontend.md (which declares types.ts the contract for backend responses), and not a duplicate: phase-69 register and confirmed_findings.json cover different FullSettings gaps (paper_max_per_sector_nav_pct, swap knobs, factor corr, starting-capital) — no register entry mentions these four field names. P2 stands: unlike the P3-downgraded phase-69 analog (alternative risk_overrides lever, documented intent to keep knobs out of UI), here the backend deliberately built full UI-facing wiring that the frontend contract never picked up; contract debt, but no live money-path impact so not P1.

confirmed.title: apiFetch maps every 404 to 'Endpoint not found... older version' and discards the parsed detail on 500s
confirmed.claim: The client assumes 404 means a missing ROUTE, but the backend uses 404 for missing RESOURCES (HTTPException(404, 'Paper portfolio not initialized'), position not found, etc.), so a fresh install shows the operator the wrong diagnosis ('backend may be running an older version') and throws away the actionable detail string; the 500 branch likewise discards the detail it just parsed (reports.py 500s embed the exception type/message).
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md role charter A2 ('API client hygiene (timeout/retry/error normalization)') + T6 tool-error guidance analogue (errors must 'clearly communicate specific and actionable improvements', anthropic.com/engineering/writing-tools-for-agents, brief source #7): the client actively replaces an accurate server detail with a wrong diagnosis.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Include the parsed `detail` in the 404 and 500 messages (e.g. `API 404 on ${path}: ${detail}`) and only suggest 'older version' when the body is FastAPI's default {"detail":"Not Found"} route-miss shape.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified verbatim at api.ts:128-133: detail is parsed (118-124) then discarded by both the 500 and 404 branches. The backend has 14 HTTPException(404) resource-miss sites (paper_trading.py:186/307/690, backtest.py:164/172/178/329/569/590/722/732, cron_dashboard_api.py:494/506/516) including fresh-install 'Paper portfolio not initialized' and 'No backtest results available', so the 'older version' diagnosis is wrong for the dominant 404 class; reports.py:48-52 confirms 500 detail embeds type/message that the client throws away. The message reaches operator-facing banners (app/page.tsx tradesError/all-failed path, manage/page.tsx depositError) despite some .catch(()=>null) callers. Not fixed (git log on api.ts), not in any phase-69/70-73 register (69's api.ts items are risk-limits wiring and the 422 [object Object] issue — different defects). P2 stands: error-normalization debt that actively misleads on routine empty-state paths, but no correctness/state impact.

confirmed.title: Promise<unknown> endpoints force per-component duplicate interfaces with conflicting nullability (Freshness vs FreshnessResponse, 2x KillSwitchState)
confirmed.claim: getPaperKillSwitchState/getPaperFreshness/getPaperCyclesHistory/getPaperTradeRationale/getPaperMfeMaeScatter return Promise<unknown>, so consumers each declare a private interface and `as`-cast: CycleHealthStrip's local Freshness marks heartbeat/thresholds NON-optional while types.ts FreshnessResponse marks them optional for the SAME compute_freshness payload (both routes share one helper per backend-api.md), and KillSwitchState is declared independently in both OpsStatusBar and KillSwitchPanel -- backend shape changes are invisible to tsc and the two frontend types already disagree.
confirmed.basis: .claude/rules/frontend.md 'Types: src/lib/types.ts -- all TypeScript interfaces for backend responses' + .claude/rules/backend-api.md 'Dual-route freshness... If you change the freshness payload shape, edit compute_freshness (the shared helper)... Don't fork' -- the frontend HAS forked the shape into three disagreeing declarations. research_brief_75.0.md T3 (unchecked casts audit).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Type the five endpoints with the canonical types.ts interfaces (reuse FreshnessResponse; add KillSwitchStateResponse mirroring paper_trading.py:504-518 incl. sod_date/thresholds), delete the local component interfaces and the `as` casts.
confirmed.verify_reason: All quoted evidence verified verbatim. Five endpoints return Promise<unknown> (api.ts:407, 459, 467 cycles:unknown[], 471, 476), forcing unchecked as-casts in OpsStatusBar.tsx:80-81, KillSwitchPanel.tsx:47, CycleHealthStrip.tsx:74. Nullability conflict confirmed: CycleHealthStrip's local Freshness (lines 14-27) marks heartbeat/thresholds/computed_at NON-optional while types.ts FreshnessResponse (1236-1254) marks them optional for the same compute_freshness payload (backend-api.md confirms one shared helper, 'Don't fork'). The two independent KillSwitchState declarations genuinely disagree (OpsStatusBar omits sod_nav/peak_nav/thresholds and per-limit breached booleans; both omit sod_date which the backend returns at paper_trading.py ~510). Violates frontend.md 'Types: src/lib/types.ts — all TypeScript interfaces for backend responses'. No neutralizing wrapper/validation; no duplicate in confirmed_findings.json or phase-69 register. P2 honest: no live crash (fields populated by compute_freshness, .catch fallbacks present) but tsc-invisible backend-shape drift with already-diverged types is real debt.

confirmed.title: tsconfig: target ES2017, skipLibCheck true, no noUncheckedIndexedAccess -- strictness ratchet behind current TS defaults
confirmed.claim: The config lacks noUncheckedIndexedAccess (now a `tsc --init` default), keeps a stale ES2017 target on an evergreen-browser SPA, and sets skipLibCheck true -- pervasive Record<string, T> index reads across the dashboard (band/color maps, meta lookups) type as T instead of T|undefined, a latent-undefined-crash class tsc currently cannot flag (verified: tsc --noEmit exits 0 today, so the ratchet can be raised from a green baseline).
confirmed.evidence:
frontend/tsconfig.json:3: "target": "ES2017",
frontend/tsconfig.json:10-11:
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
Example unchecked index pattern, frontend/src/components/CycleHealthStrip.tsx:40-41:
const BAND_CLS: Record<string, string> = {
  green: "bg-emerald-500/15 text-emerald-300 border-emerald-500/30",
Deterministic baseline: `./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit --incremental false` -> EXIT 0.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T3 rule 6 ('tsconfig headroom: strict: true present but target: ES2017 (stale...), skipLibCheck: true, missing noUncheckedIndexedAccess') and recency scan item 11 ('tsc --init now defaults noUncheckedIndexedAccess + exactOptionalPropertyTypes -- the strictness ratchet moved'); official reference https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig#noUncheckedIndexedAccess.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Bump target to ES2022, enable noUncheckedIndexedAccess (fix surfaced index-access errors with ?? fallbacks -- the codebase already uses the `?? default` idiom in most maps), and evaluate dropping skipLibCheck once deps are clean.
confirmed.verify_reason: Evidence verified exactly: tsconfig.json:3 target ES2017, :10 skipLibCheck true, :11 strict true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess absent; BAND_CLS Record<string,string> at CycleHealthStrip.tsx:40; tsc --noEmit --incremental false re-run exits 0 (green baseline confirmed). The specific BAND_CLS site is runtime-guarded (line 180: `BAND_CLS[p.band] || BAND_CLS.unknown`), but the guard is invisible to tsc and the pattern class is pervasive (150 `Record<string` hits in frontend/src, not all guarded), so the compiler-gap claim holds in context. Not a duplicate: no phase-69..73 register item covers tsconfig; only prior mentions are research_brief_75.0.md itself. Caveat weakening one leg: ES2017 target is the create-next-app scaffold default and Next/SWC emit is governed by browserslist, so the target bump is near-cosmetic — the actionable core is noUncheckedIndexedAccess. P2 debt is the honest severity: real latent-crash class, no live defect demonstrated.

confirmed.title: Backend-authoritative sector_breakdown in /portfolio is untyped and unread; UI re-derives sector split client-side
confirmed.claim: paper_trading.py ships sector_breakdown in the /portfolio response specifically 'so the Risk Monitor and any future monitoring... read the same numbers', but getPaperPortfolio's return type omits the key and zero frontend references exist -- the donut/positions views instead re-derive sector weights client-side from useTickerMeta + market_value with their own denominator, exactly the divergence the field was shipped to close.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md A2 charter (types.ts drift field-by-field) + phase-69 register row 73's documented pattern of duplicate client derivations silently diverging (this specific field/consumer pair is not in the phase-69/70 registers -- verified by grep of register.md and confirmed_findings.json).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add sector_breakdown: Record<string, {count: number; weight_pct: number; tickers: string[]}> to the getPaperPortfolio return type and have the donut/Risk views consume it (fallback to client derivation only when absent), or delete the field from the backend response if the client derivation is the accepted source.
confirmed.verify_reason: CONFIRMED. paper_trading.py:190-193 comment and :238 response field exist as quoted; api.ts:321 return type omits sector_breakdown; grep shows ZERO frontend consumers, and the comment's other stated consumers (Slack digest, monitoring) do not read it either — formatters.py:490 references it only in a comment, backend/monitoring has no hits, and the sole 'readers' are test fixtures stubbing {}. positions/page.tsx:106-128 re-derives sector bar + donut slices from tickerMeta + mvUsd with its own denominator (invested MV, market-scoped) while the backend divides by total_nav (cash-inclusive), so the two numbers genuinely diverge in semantics. Not in phase-69/70 registers (row 73 is the polling-clock divergence, a different mechanism). Caveat for fixer: client derivation is market-scoped with USD conversion, so consuming the backend field is not drop-in; deleting the dead field is the cheaper option. P3 honest — dead-code debt, no live money-path impact.

confirmed.title: ReportSummary omits analysis_path, so lite/full provenance shipped for the away-week incident is invisible in the UI
confirmed.claim: backend ReportSummary added analysis_path ('lite' | 'full' | None) in phase-60.1 because 'the away week showed identical-looking 7.0/10 digest rows from the 2-call lite wrapper', but the frontend ReportSummary type omits the field and no component reads it -- the operator still cannot distinguish degraded lite digests from full analyses in the reports list.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md A2 charter (field-by-field drift on main endpoints); GET /api/reports/ declares response_model=list[ReportSummary] (backend/api/reports.py:28) so the field is guaranteed present in every row.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add analysis_path?: 'lite' | 'full' | null to frontend ReportSummary and render a small provenance badge on lite rows in the reports list (pairs with the existing degraded null-score handling).
confirmed.verify_reason: Backend ReportSummary (backend/api/models.py:107) ships analysis_path with real data — bigquery_client.py:268 selects JSON_VALUE(full_report_json,'$._path') AS analysis_path in get_recent_reports, and reports.py:28 serves it via response_model=list[ReportSummary]. Frontend ReportSummary (frontend/src/lib/types.ts:116) omits the field and grep shows zero analysis_path hits in frontend/src; consumers (reports/page.tsx, reports-columns.tsx, RecentReportsTable.tsx, ReportCompareDrawer.tsx) never render lite/full provenance, so lite digest rows are indistinguishable in the UI. The phase-61.2 degraded/null-score handling WAS ported to the frontend type but the phase-60.1 provenance field was not, indicating omission rather than intent. No duplicate in confirmed_findings.json, harness_proposals.json, audit_phase69, or design_pack_73. Purely missing UI visibility (no runtime/type breakage from the extra field), so P3 is the honest severity.

confirmed.title: getSeedStability(): Promise<any> leaks into useState<any> -- unchecked property access chain in HarnessDashboard
confirmed.claim: The only explicit `any` in the API client (eslint-disabled) propagates into component state typed useState<any>, so every downstream access (seedStability.status, .results?.verdict) is unchecked by tsc; a backend shape change or partial payload renders silently wrong instead of failing the build.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md A2 charter ('TS strictness (any-leaks, unchecked casts...)'); the project's own eslint config treats no-explicit-any as an error (the inline disable is the tell).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Define a SeedStabilityResponse interface in types.ts (status: 'not_started'|'running'|'complete'|'crashed'; results?: {verdict?: string; ...}) mirroring the /api/backtest/harness/seed-stability payload and drop both `any`s.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in code: api.ts:607-609 has the eslint-disabled Promise<any> (the only explicit any in the API client; every sibling harness endpoint is properly typed), and HarnessDashboard.tsx:197 stores it in useState<any>, with ~20 unchecked property accesses across lines 291-391 including inline structural casts at 354/391 that re-declare the undeclared shape. No SeedStabilityResponse type exists in types.ts; no wrapper/guard restores tsc coverage (optional chaining only softens runtime, not type-checking). Not a duplicate of any phase-69/70/71/72/73 register item (confirmed_findings.json, harness_proposals.json, audit_phase69 all clean). P3 is the honest severity: pure TS-strictness debt within the 75.0 A2 charter, no live correctness impact, cheap well-specified fix.

confirmed.title: Response-shape interfaces scattered in api.ts (RiskLimit*, Sovereign*, AgentMap*), inline 20-field round-trips type, lib->components type import, dead AuthUser
confirmed.claim: api.ts defines ~12 backend-response interfaces (RiskLimitEntry/RiskLimitsResponse, 8 Sovereign*/Strategy*, 4 AgentMap*), declares a 20-field anonymous inline type for /round-trips, and imports the GoLiveGate response type FROM a component file -- all violating the project rule that backend-response types live in types.ts, which also carries a fully-dead AuthUser interface (zero references anywhere); type discovery and drift-checking are fragmented across three locations.
confirmed.basis: .claude/rules/frontend.md (project standard, quoted above) + research_brief_75.0.md A2 charter item 'unused/duplicated types'. Functional consequence: the phase-70 risk-limits fix landed its types in api.ts because the canonical home was not enforced, deepening the split.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Move the api.ts response interfaces and the inline round-trips shape into types.ts, relocate GoLiveGate from the component into types.ts (component re-imports it), and delete AuthUser.
confirmed.verify_reason: All evidence verified. api.ts defines ~17 backend-response interfaces (RiskLimitEntry/RiskLimitsResponse at 264/275, 7 Sovereign* + 4 Strategy* at 658-733, 4 AgentMap* at 754-797), an anonymous 20-field inline response type for getPaperRoundTrips at 368-399, and imports GoLiveGate from a component file at 401 (GoLiveGate defined in GoLiveGateWidget.tsx:7 and consumed by OpsStatusBar too — genuine lib->components inversion). .claude/rules/frontend.md:16 states verbatim that types.ts holds "all TypeScript interfaces for backend responses", so the project rule is violated, not intentional. AuthUser (types.ts:45) has zero references in the entire frontend tree beyond its definition — fully dead. No neutralizing wrapper exists; this is compile-time organization only. Not a duplicate: phase-69/70 register items touching api.ts concern missing risk-limits UI, 422 error UX, and missing max_drawdown_pct — none cover type-location fragmentation, GoLiveGate placement, or AuthUser. The phase-70.1 comment above RiskLimitEntry confirms the drift narrative (fix landed types in api.ts, not types.ts). P3 is honest: zero runtime consequence, pure maintainability polish.

confirmed.title: Error contract is message-string matching: 5 pages branch on includes('Cannot reach') against api.ts prose
confirmed.claim: apiFetch throws plain Error with no status/code field, so retry-guidance UI in five pages is coupled to exact message prose ('Cannot reach backend at ...'); any reword of the api.ts message silently disables those affordances, and components cannot distinguish 401/404/422/500 programmatically.
confirmed.evidence:
frontend/src/lib/api.ts:100-103:
    if (msg.includes("Failed to fetch") || msg.includes("NetworkError")) {
      throw new Error(
        `Cannot reach backend at ${API_BASE}. ` +
        "Make sure the FastAPI server is running (uvicorn backend.main:app --port 8000)."
Consumers matching the prose:
frontend/src/app/page.tsx:367: {loadError.includes("Cannot reach") && (
frontend/src/app/signals/page.tsx:101: {error.includes("Cannot reach backend") && (
frontend/src/app/settings/page.tsx:531 / backtest/page.tsx:770 / paper-trading/layout.tsx:446: same pattern.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md A2 charter ('API client hygiene... error normalization'); the anti-pattern is the client-side mirror of T6's 'opaque error codes' rule (brief source #7) -- machine-branchable semantics encoded only in human prose.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Introduce `class ApiError extends Error { status?: number; kind: 'network'|'timeout'|'http' }` thrown by apiFetch; components branch on err.kind/status instead of message substrings (messages stay for display).
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in code: apiFetch (frontend/src/lib/api.ts:90-135) throws only plain Error objects — network (100-104), timeout (93), 401/422/500/404 (112-134) — with no status/kind field, so semantics live solely in prose. All five cited consumers branch on that prose: page.tsx:367, signals/page.tsx:101, settings/page.tsx:531, backtest/page.tsx, paper-trading/layout.tsx:446 use includes("Cannot reach...") to gate the uvicorn retry-guidance hint, and page.tsx:241 re-hardcodes the same prose as a fallback. No ApiError class or normalization layer exists in frontend/src; nothing pins the string contract, so any reword silently disables the affordances. Not blessed by .claude/rules/frontend.md, not fixed, and no phase-69/70/71/72/73 register item covers it (prior api.ts items concern risk-limits wiring and 422 detail rendering, distinct defects). Consequence is limited to a lost hint line and unbranchable errors — polish/debt, so P3 is the honest severity.

confirmed.title: apiFetch: caller-supplied signal silently disables the 30s timeout, and every AbortError is reported as a 30s timeout
confirmed.claim: `signal: init?.signal ?? controller.signal` means any future caller passing an abort signal loses the documented 30s watchdog entirely (the timeout controller aborts a controller not wired to the fetch), and the catch classifies ALL AbortErrors as 'timed out after 30 seconds' even when the abort was external -- latent today (no current caller passes a signal, verified by reading all 70+ exported wrappers) but a trap for the first component that adds request cancellation.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md A2 charter ('API client hygiene (timeout/retry/error normalization)') and .claude/rules/frontend.md which documents the invariant this code can silently break: 'API client: src/lib/api.ts -- ... 30s AbortController timeout on all requests'.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Use AbortSignal.any([controller.signal, init.signal]) when a caller signal is present (or addEventListener bridge for ES2017 target), and distinguish timeout vs external abort via a controller-scoped flag before claiming '30 seconds'.
confirmed.verify_reason: Evidence verified verbatim at api.ts:79-80, 88, 92-96. The `init?.signal ?? controller.signal` fallback means a caller-supplied signal fully replaces the timeout controller's signal, so the 30s watchdog (line 80) would abort an unwired controller and never cancel the fetch; and the catch at 92-96 labels ALL AbortErrors as a 30s timeout, which would misreport external aborts. Latency claim confirmed: grep of frontend/src shows zero AbortController/AbortSignal usage outside api.ts and no wrapper passes signal, so no live impact today. Not neutralized by any guard; .claude/rules/frontend.md documents the '30s AbortController timeout on all requests' invariant this code can silently break. No duplicate in confirmed_findings.json or phase-69/70/71/72/73 registers. P3 is the honest severity: real, latent, trivially fixable (AbortSignal.any or listener bridge + timeout-vs-abort flag), worth fixing as hardening under the A2 API-client-hygiene charter.

confirmed.title: AllSignals sub-types declare non-optional structured fields the backend replaces with {signal:'ERROR', summary} stubs on failure
confirmed.claim: Every one of the 12 signals in GET /api/signals/{ticker} degrades to a two-key error stub on tool failure (yfinance rate limits make this routine), but SectorData/InsiderData/OptionsData declare stock_returns/peers/cluster_buys etc. as non-optional -- existing consumers already distrust the type (`|| {}` and `?.` workarounds), and any new consumer trusting the declared type (e.g. Object.entries(data.stock_returns)) crashes exactly when a signal errors.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T5 API10 ('third-party API responses trusted? -- phase-69 found real instances') applied to the frontend contract: the degraded shape is a designed backend behavior the TS types do not model. Distinct from phase-69 register items (none cover signals-page types).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Model the union: type SignalResult<T> = T | { signal: 'ERROR'; summary: string }, or mark the structured fields optional in SectorData/InsiderData/OptionsData/MonteCarloData/AnomalyData so tsc forces the guards consumers already hand-write.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified: backend/api/signals.py:64-66 returns a two-key {signal:'ERROR', summary} stub for any of the 12 gathered signals and puts it straight into the AllSignals response, while types.ts declares SectorData (line 214, fields 220-234), InsiderData (194-203) and OptionsData (205-212) with non-optional structured fields typed onto AllSignals.insider/options/sector. The defensive workarounds quoted (SectorDashboard.tsx:23 `|| {}`, :77-81 `?.`) exist, and useEnrichmentSignals.ts's pick() coercion plus the `signal !== "ERROR"` guard at signals/page.tsx:127-137 and the `as Record<string, string>` cast at :128 all prove consumers already distrust the declared type. No live crash today (all current consumers guarded; no consumer reads cluster_buys/unusual_activity), which caps this at P3, but the TS contract genuinely fails to model a designed, routine backend degradation, so tsc cannot enforce the guards. Not covered by any phase-69/70-73 register item (closest is the unrelated max_drawdown_pct phantom-field finding). One drift in the suggested fix: MonteCarloData/AnomalyData interfaces do not exist (those AllSignals fields are already Record<string, unknown>); the fix only needs InsiderData/OptionsData/SectorData or a SignalResult<T> union.

confirmed.title: Ingest dedup guards swallow all exceptions, silently re-inserting duplicate historical bars
confirmed.claim: _get_existing_price_dates and _get_existing_fundamentals catch ALL exceptions and return an empty set with zero logging; ingest_prices then treats 'nothing exists' as truth and streams every row in the requested range into historical_prices via insert_rows_json (line 167), producing duplicate (ticker,date) bars. The backtest preload cache (backend/backtest/cache.py:123-126 groupby/set_index) does not dedupe, so duplicated bars silently distort feature building, mark-to-market, and Sharpe/DSR inputs downstream.
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T4 (streaming inserts are append-only best-effort; batch/dedup discipline) + T8 rule 2 evidence discipline; official https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/best-practices-costs (streaming per-row semantics). Failure trigger is any transient BQ error/timeout during the existence check while the yfinance download succeeded — the exact BQ-timeout class the phase-69 register documents as live (register.md:27).
confirmed.suggested_fix: In both _get_existing_* helpers: log the exception and FAIL CLOSED (re-raise or skip the batch) instead of returning set() — a failed dedup check must abort insertion, not authorize blanket re-insert. Longer term, make the write idempotent (MERGE on (ticker,date) or load into a staging table + MERGE).
confirmed.verify_reason: Verbatim evidence confirmed: _get_existing_price_dates (lines 88-92), _get_existing_fundamentals (186-190), and even _get_existing_macro (277-278) all catch bare Exception and return set() with zero logging; ingest_prices then streams every row via insert_rows_json (line 167). No guard neutralizes it in context: daily_price_refresh.run_production (backend/slack_bot/jobs/daily_price_refresh.py:82) runs daily over the S&P 500 with a 7-day lookback and its docstring explicitly relies on this dedup for idempotency, so one transient BQ error on the existence query silently re-inserts ~500 tickers x ~5 days of duplicate bars. Downstream claim verified: cache.py:123-126 groupby/set_index performs no dedup, so duplicate (ticker,date) rows corrupt feature building, mark-to-market NAV, and Sharpe/DSR. Schema check (migrate_backtest_data.py: date is STRING) confirms the normal dedup path works, so the defect is precisely the exception path. Not fixed, not sanctioned by .claude/rules, and no duplicate in audit_phase69/register.md or away_ops/defect_register.md (congress.py:270 is a different file/mechanism; research_brief_75.0 T4 is this audit's own scope note). P1 honest: latent trigger (transient BQ failure) but silent, permanent corruption of the frozen historical table with a daily production trigger surface.

confirmed.title: f-string SQL value interpolation in core BQ client and data_ingestion despite parameterization convention
confirmed.claim: get_agent_memories builds its WHERE clause and LIMIT by f-string interpolation of Python values into SQL, and data_ingestion interpolates a quoted ticker list under a comment falsely claiming 'Use parameterized IN clause'. This violates the repo's own parameterized-query convention (QueryJobConfig used at 20+ sibling sites), defeats BigQuery query-plan caching, and leaves a latent injection surface (currently only internal callers: orchestrator.py:735 passes no agent_type; tickers come from the screener/config — so latent, not live). A ticker containing a quote also breaks the data_ingestion query, feeding the data-bq-01 silent-duplicate path.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/db/bigquery_client.py:500-508:

            query = f"""
                SELECT agent_type, ticker, situation, lesson, created_at
                FROM `{table}`
                {where}
                ORDER BY created_at DESC
                LIMIT {int(limit)}
            """
            rows = list(self.client.query(query).result())
backend/backtest/data_ingestion.py:81-82:

        ticker_list = ", ".join(f"'{t}'" for t in tickers[:100])
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T4 rule 6: 'Parameterized queries via QueryJobConfig(query_parameters=...) — bigquery_client.py is broadly compliant — auditors verify NO f-string SQL interpolation anywhere else'. This is the counterexample, inside the core client itself. Official: https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/best-practices-costs (cache-defeat) + OWASP injection hygiene (brief T5).
confirmed.suggested_fix: get_agent_memories: use ScalarQueryParameter for agent_type and @limit (mirror get_recent_reports at :276-280). data_ingestion: use ArrayQueryParameter + IN UNNEST(@tickers) exactly as backend/backtest/cache.py:103,108-112 already does in the same subsystem.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified verbatim at both sites. bigquery_client.py:500-508: get_agent_memories interpolates agent_type into WHERE via f-string and calls self.client.query(query) with no job_config, while 24 sibling sites in the same file use QueryJobConfig (get_recent_reports ~:276-280 parameterizes @limit exactly as the fix suggests). data_ingestion.py:81-86: comment 'Use parameterized IN clause' is false — quoted ticker list is f-string-joined into IN(...), and the try/except at :88-92 returns set() on query failure, feeding the data-bq-01 silent-duplicate path; backend/backtest/cache.py:103-112 in the same subsystem already uses ArrayQueryParameter + IN UNNEST(@tickers). Latent-not-live framing checks out: sole get_agent_memories caller is orchestrator.py:735 with no agent_type, limit is int()-cast, and tickers are internal (screener/config, sanitized per security.md). Not a duplicate — phase-69 agent_memories register items concern the tz-aware learning-loop crash, not SQL construction. P2 (convention debt + latent surface, no live break) is the honest severity.

confirmed.title: CLAUDE.md 30s BQ timeout rule unenforced: 31 untimed .result() calls including money-path DML
confirmed.claim: The repo's own rule (CLAUDE.md:35 'BQ timeout: 30s on all fallback queries', enforced-with-comment at bigquery_client.py:529-533) is applied at only ~9 call sites; 31 non-test .result() calls carry no timeout, including the money-path DML runner _run_dml_with_retry (executes all paper_positions/paper_trades MERGEs), the portfolio INSERT (:571), get_paper_positions (:580), and 13 more across services/api (paper_trader.py:1245, cycle_health.py:474, sortino.py:114 — an explicit fallback query, api/paper_trading.py:1127, performance_api.py:82, pead_signal.py:342, sector_calendars.py:200, skill_optimizer.py:173/185, cost_budget_watcher.py:104, slot_accounting.py:139, harness_autoresearch.py:196, monthly_approval_api.py:141). A dropped connection can hang .result() indefinitely, stranding the autonomous-loop worker thread (asyncio timeouts cannot cancel a blocked thread) and any lock it holds.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/db/bigquery_client.py:536-541:
    def _run_dml_with_retry(self, query: str, job_config, max_retries: int = 3) -> None:
        """Run a DML query with retry for BQ streaming buffer conflicts."""
        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
            try:
                self.client.query(query, job_config=job_config).result()
                return
Contrast :529-533 (same file):
        # phase-23.1.20: enforce CLAUDE.md "BQ timeout: 30s" so a hung BQ job
        # doesn't strand a thread indefinitely. ...
        rows = list(self.client.query(query, job_config=job_config).result(timeout=30))
Census: grep '.result(' non-test = 67 with timeout, 79 without (31 of those are BQ jobs; remainder ThreadPool futures).
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T4 rule 3 ('Every client.query(...) and .result(...) carries a timeout — repo convention 30s') + snippet source S9 https://github.com/googleapis/python-bigquery/issues/2310 (client can hang indefinitely on dropped connections) + CLAUDE.md:35.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add timeout=30 to every BQ .result() in bigquery_client.py (DML paths may warrant 60s), then sweep the 13 external sites. Consider a thin helper (self._query(sql, cfg, timeout=30)) so new call sites inherit the rule; also note google-api-core deprecated retry 'deadline' in favor of 'timeout' (brief S8) when touching retry configs.
confirmed.verify_reason: Confirmed. Line 540 bare .result() in _run_dml_with_retry verified verbatim, directly contrasting the phase-23.1.20 comment at :529-533 that enforces timeout=30 for exactly this hazard ('a hung BQ job doesn't strand a thread indefinitely... the 30s here is the ceiling for the worker thread itself' — the repo itself concedes asyncio timeouts can't cancel the blocked thread). _run_dml_with_retry executes the money-path paper_portfolio DELETE and paper_positions/paper_trades MERGEs (:557,:640,:656); lines 571/580/590 also bare. All 13 external sites verified at exact claimed lines (5 file paths were misdescribed as services/ — actual: backend/metrics/sortino.py, backend/agents/skill_optimizer.py, backend/slack_bot/jobs/cost_budget_watcher.py, backend/autoresearch/slot_accounting.py, backend/api/harness_autoresearch.py — but line numbers exact). Census consistent: ~35 untimed vs 30 timed non-test BQ .result() backend-wide; 18 untimed vs 7 timed in bigquery_client.py alone. Not a duplicate: phase-69 register timeout items are distinct defects (kill_switch nav=0.0 phantom breach, LLM ThreadPoolExecutor join); no phase-70/71/72/73 register or harness_proposals item covers a .result() timeout sweep. P2 honest: latent robustness debt needing a dropped connection to trigger, not live correctness; money-path exposure keeps it above P3.

confirmed.title: Bulk historical backfills use per-row-billed streaming insertAll instead of free batch load jobs
confirmed.claim: data_ingestion streams thousands of backfill rows (prices :167, fundamentals :257, macro :324) through insert_rows_json — the legacy tabledata.insertAll API with per-row charges and a streaming buffer that blocks DML for up to 90 minutes. These are offline bulk loads with zero latency requirement: load_table_from_json/dataframe would be free and buffer-less. The repo already pays the operational cost of this choice: bigquery_client.py:536-547 exists solely to retry 'streaming buffer conflict' DML failures with sleeps up to 20s inside the trading loop.
confirmed.evidence:
backend/backtest/data_ingestion.py:164-169:

            for j in range(0, len(rows), _BQ_BATCH):
                sub = rows[j:j + _BQ_BATCH]
                errors = self.client.insert_rows_json(table, sub)
                if errors:
                    logger.error(f"BQ insert errors (prices): {errors[:3]}")
backend/db/bigquery_client.py:537,543-545:
        """Run a DML query with retry for BQ streaming buffer conflicts."""
                if "streaming buffer" in str(e).lower() and attempt < max_retries:
                    wait = 2 ** attempt * 5  # 5s, 10s, 20s
                    logger.warning(f"BQ streaming buffer conflict ...")
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T4 rule 5 + source #4 https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/best-practices-costs ('streaming inserts incur per-row charges; batch loading is more economical') + recency scan item 4: official guidance 'For new projects, we recommend using the BigQuery Storage Write API instead of the tabledata.insertAll method' (googleapis/python-bigquery archived Mar 2026).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Switch the three data_ingestion bulk paths to client.load_table_from_json(rows, table, job_config=LoadJobConfig(schema=...)) — free, atomic per job, no streaming buffer. Leave low-volume event-log streaming sites (intel/*, meta_evolution/*) as-is or queue them for Storage Write API later; they are latency-justified.
confirmed.verify_reason: Evidence verified exactly: bulk backfills stream via insert_rows_json at data_ingestion.py:167/257/324 with no guard, and load_table_from_json would be free and buffer-less; not a duplicate (phase-69 register item on streaming is congress.py dedup, different defect). Downgraded P2->P3: the historical_* tables are FROZEN with zero DML anywhere in the repo, so the 90-min streaming-buffer DML block cannot bite; queries see buffer rows immediately; billing impact is cents per full backfill. The finding's 'repo already pays the cost' link is misattributed — bigquery_client.py:536 _run_dml_with_retry protects paper-trading tables whose conflicts come from other streaming sites (bigquery_client.py:251/394/403/429/492), not data_ingestion. Real, worth fixing cheaply, but polish-grade.

confirmed.title: No maximum_bytes_billed on any query — runaway-scan cost guard absent repo-wide
confirmed.claim: grep for maximum_bytes_billed across backend/ and scripts/ returns zero hits: no QueryJobConfig anywhere sets the official pre-charge kill switch, so a mis-written query (e.g. an accidental cross join or unfiltered scan as tables grow) bills in full. This compounds with data-bq-04 (unpartitioned tables) and the phase-69-documented coupling of bytes-billed into the trading-halt budget breaker: a single runaway scan both costs money and can halt trading for the day.
confirmed.evidence:
Empirical gate (grep, 2026-07-19): `grep -rn "maximum_bytes_billed" backend/ scripts/ --include="*.py"` -> 0 hits.
Representative default-less config, backend/db/bigquery_client.py:278-280:
        job_config = bigquery.QueryJobConfig(
            query_parameters=[bigquery.ScalarQueryParameter("limit", "INT64", limit)]
        )
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T4 rule 4 (flagged as candidate hardening by the research gate, confirmed still absent) + source #4 https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/best-practices-costs ('if the number of estimated bytes is beyond the limit, then the query fails without incurring a charge').
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add a module-level default (e.g. _MAX_BYTES = 5 * 2**30) applied in a shared QueryJobConfig factory in bigquery_client.py and the standalone client constructors (intel/, autoresearch/, metrics/); allow the backtest bulk preloads a higher explicit ceiling.
confirmed.verify_reason: Reproduced the grep: zero maximum_bytes_billed hits across all Python in the repo; 16 files construct QueryJobConfig and none sets the pre-charge cap. Quoted evidence at bigquery_client.py:278-280 matches verbatim. No neutralizing guard exists: the only cost controls are post-hoc (cost_budget_watcher.py:96-98 and sovereign_api.py:296 sum total_bytes_billed from INFORMATION_SCHEMA after billing), and per the phase-69 register that metered spend feeds the trading-halt budget breaker — so a runaway scan bills in full and can halt trading, exactly as claimed. Not a duplicate: phase-69's llm_client.py:431 item is the mislabeled-metering defect, not the missing pre-charge cap; the research_brief_75.0 T4-rule-4 mention is this audit's own seed, not a prior register entry. P3 is honest: preventive cost hardening with no live defect today.

confirmed.title: SELECT * on the 88-column analysis_results table (and 10 paper-table sites) scans full_report_json on every lookup
confirmed.claim: get_report's three variants (:319, :334, :344) SELECT * from the 88-column analysis_results table, which includes the large full_report_json blob — on an unpartitioned table every call scans every column of every row, and outcome_tracker.evaluate_all_pending calls get_report per pending row (loop amplification). Ten more SELECT * sites cover the paper_* tables (:523, :577, :584, :698, :956, :975, :1041) and sovereign_api.py:229 (SELECT * ... LIMIT 100 — LIMIT does not cap bytes). Small tables today, but each is schema-drift-fragile (column adds silently change payload shapes) and violates the explicit projection discipline cache.py already adopted with measured savings (phase-53.3 comment: 21% bytes cut).

backend/db/bigquery_client.py:318-324:

backend/backtest/cache.py:153-154 (the in-repo counter-pattern):

confirmed.verify_reason: All cited SELECT * sites verified verbatim (bigquery_client.py:319/334/344 get_report variants, paper-table sites :523/:577/:584/:698/:956/:975/:1041, sovereign_api.py:229; finding even missed :445). Loop amplification confirmed: outcome_tracker.evaluate_all_pending calls bq.get_report per pending row (outcome_tracker.py:118). cache.py:153 phase-53.3 explicit-projection counter-pattern exists as quoted. Not a duplicate: the phase-69 register item touching bigquery_client.py:955-960 is a STRING-vs-TIMESTAMP 400 error, not SELECT * cost. Caveat weakening (but not killing) the finding: get_report's caller consumes full_report_json (the dominant column), so byte savings there are minimal, and research_brief_63.2 already established the paper_* tables are tiny — the value is schema-drift hygiene and projection discipline, which is exactly P3 polish.
