confirmed.title: Unauthenticated POST mutates HITL champion-challenger approval gate
confirmed.claim: `/api/harness/monthly-approval` is listed in `_PUBLIC_PATHS`, so the auth middleware skips it for ALL methods including the state-changing POST `/{month_key}`. Any client that can reach the port (no session token, no operator identity) can approve or reject a monthly champion-challenger deployment and write a BQ audit row, defeating the human-in-the-loop governance gate (OWASP API5 Broken Function Level Authorization).
confirmed.evidence: main.py:406-435 — `_PUBLIC_PATHS = (` ... `"/api/harness/monthly-approval",` (:412) ... and the gate `if request.method != "OPTIONS" and not any(path.startswith(p) for p in _PUBLIC_PATHS):` (:435) means `POST /api/harness/monthly-approval/2026-07` matches the prefix and skips `get_current_user`. The handler `post_monthly_approval(month_key, body)` (monthly_approval_api.py:184) calls `record_approval(month_key, status=action, bq_fn=_default_bq_logger)` (:196) which mutates `monthly_approval_state.json` and inserts into `strategy_deployments_log`. No auth or operator check anywhere in the handler.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T5 (OWASP API Security Top 10 2023, API5 Function-level authz / API8 misconfig) anchored to owasp.org/API-Security/editions/2023/en/0x11-t10/; internal-recon seed main.py:405+ `_PUBLIC_PATHS` doc-drift; .claude/rules/security.md:11 documents only /api/health,/api/auth,/docs,/openapi.json,/redoc as auth-skip. The other public harness routes are read-only GET dashboards; this is the only public prefix carrying a POST mutator.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove `/api/harness/monthly-approval` from `_PUBLIC_PATHS` (the frontend already sends the NextAuth session cookie via credentials:include), OR keep GET public but require `get_current_user` (and ideally an operator-email check) inside `post_monthly_approval`. Do not gate a governance state transition on network reachability alone.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in code: main.py:412 puts "/api/harness/monthly-approval" in _PUBLIC_PATHS and the :435 gate skips get_current_user for all methods matching the prefix, so POST /api/harness/monthly-approval/{month_key} is unauthenticated. Handler post_monthly_approval (backend/api/monthly_approval_api.py:184-210) has no auth/operator check and calls record_approval(...) which mutates monthly_approval_state and writes a strategy_deployments_log BQ row. Not intentional: .claude/rules/security.md:11 lists only health/auth/docs as auth-skips, and the phase-15.3 harness_log entry that added the prefix gave no POST justification — the sibling /api/jobs/status entry explicitly kept its POST auth-gated, showing public-GET-only was the intended convention. Not a duplicate: phase-69 register entry on monthly_approval_api.py:196 is a different (refuted) defect about missing status_update_fn; confirmed_findings.json has no _PUBLIC_PATHS/monthly-approval item. P1 is honest: real API5 broken function-level authorization on a HITL governance mutation, mitigated to latent-serious because uvicorn binds 127.0.0.1 by default (CLAUDE.md:9) and CORS limits browser origins to localhost/Tailscale — but any local process or tailnet client can approve/reject a champion-challenger deployment with zero identity.

confirmed.title: _PUBLIC_PATHS has drifted to 13+ unauthenticated prefixes exposing business data
confirmed.claim: `_PUBLIC_PATHS` now whitelists 16 prefixes as auth-skip, far beyond the 5 documented in .claude/rules/security.md. Unauthenticated GETs expose NAV/red-line series and strategy leaderboard (`/api/sovereign`), per-ticker enrichment signals (`/api/signals`), freshness/latency (`/api/observability`), spend (`/api/cost-budget`), and job status. Because matching is `path.startswith(p)`, every route under those routers is public. This is undocumented attack surface (OWASP API8 Security Misconfiguration + API9 Inventory) and a config-vs-doc drift.
confirmed.evidence: main.py:406-423 — `_PUBLIC_PATHS = ( "/api/health", "/api/changelog", "/api/auth", "/api/cost-budget", "/api/jobs/status", "/api/harness/monthly-approval", "/api/harness/demotion-audit", "/api/harness/weekly-ledger", "/api/harness/candidate-space", "/api/harness/results-distribution", "/api/signals", "/api/observability", "/api/sovereign", "/docs", "/openapi.json", "/redoc", )`. security.md:11 — `Auth middleware skips: /api/health, /api/auth, /docs, /openapi.json, /redoc`. sovereign_api.py:319/360 return RedLine/Leaderboard; signals.py:53 `@router.get("/{ticker}")`.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md internal-recon seed 'main.py:405+ _PUBLIC_PATHS ... unauthenticated surface beyond the 3 paths documented in .claude/rules/security.md -> doc/config drift AND API9-inventory audit item'; T5 OWASP API8/API9.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Reconcile the list: for each prefix decide public-by-design vs accidental, drop the rest so those routers require `get_current_user`, and update security.md to match. At minimum move the sensitive read surfaces (sovereign, signals, cost-budget, observability) behind auth given Tailscale is not an auth boundary.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified: main.py:406-423 whitelists 16 auth-skip prefixes vs the 5 documented in .claude/rules/security.md:11; middleware at main.py:435 matches via path.startswith so entire routers are public. Sovereign/signals/observability/cost-budget routes have no per-route Depends(get_current_user), so NAV/red-line, leaderboard, per-ticker signals, spend, and freshness data are readable with no token by any LAN/tailnet peer (server binds 0.0.0.0; CORS does not stop non-browser clients). Sensitive prefixes were added in feature commit 1122a021 with no public-by-design doc and security.md never updated — genuine config-vs-doc drift plus undocumented attack surface. No neutralizing guard (DEV_LOCALHOST_BYPASS is localhost-only and orthogonal). Not in phase-69 register nor any prior queue; briefs 62.5/63.2 mention the fact but queue no fix; the 75.0 brief reference is this audit's own seed. Severity capped at P2: all exposed routes are read-only (no POST/PUT/DELETE under those routers) and reachability is Tailscale/LAN-bounded, so it is misconfiguration debt, not a live P0/P1 compromise.

confirmed.title: CORS allows publicly-routable 100.x origins with credentials (not just Tailscale CGNAT)
confirmed.claim: The CORS origin regex `100\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+` matches the entire 100.0.0.0/8 space, but Tailscale CGNAT is only 100.64.0.0/10. Publicly-routable ranges (e.g. 100.0-100.63 and 100.128-100.255, which include real internet hosts) are accepted as trusted origins with `allow_credentials=True`, `allow_methods=["*"]`, `allow_headers=["*"]`. The manual CORS echo in the 401 branch is even looser (`origin.startswith("http://100.")`). A page served from a non-Tailscale 100.x origin could make credentialed cross-origin reads.
confirmed.evidence: main.py:399 — `allow_origin_regex=r"^http://(localhost|100\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+):\d+$",` with :400 `allow_credentials=True`, :401 `allow_methods=["*"]`, :402 `allow_headers=["*"]`. main.py:451 — `or (origin.startswith("http://100.") and origin.count(".") == 3)` reflects `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` for any 100.x.x.x.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md internal-recon seed main.py:397-403 (CORS scoped but audit against security.md §CORS) + T5 OWASP API8 (CORS misconfiguration). Tailscale CGNAT range 100.64.0.0/10 is the intended trust set.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Tighten the regex to the CGNAT block, e.g. `100\.(6[4-9]|[7-9]\d|1[01]\d|12[0-7])\.\d+\.\d+`, and apply the same narrowing to the manual echo at main.py:449-454 (share one predicate). Keeps localhost + real Tailscale, drops public 100.x.
confirmed.verify_reason: Evidence verified exactly: main.py:399 regex `100\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+` matches all of 100.0.0.0/8 (Tailscale CGNAT is only 100.64.0.0/10; publicly-routable blocks like AWS 100.20.0.0/14 pass), with allow_credentials=True and wildcard methods/headers; the manual 401-branch echo at main.py:449-454 is even looser (any "http://100." with 3 dots) and reflects origin + Allow-Credentials:true. security.md §CORS confirms intent is "Tailscale IPs", so over-breadth is unintended, not fixed, and no phase-69..73 register duplicate exists (only this audit's own seed at research_brief_75.0.md:290). One caveat: the headline "credentialed cross-origin reads" claim is overstated — auth rides an HttpOnly NextAuth cookie with default SameSite=Lax (no sameSite override anywhere in frontend app code), so modern browsers won't attach it to a cross-site fetch from a public 100.x origin, and the Bearer token is unreachable. But the misconfig retains a real consequence: _PUBLIC_PATHS (main.py:406-423) exposes unauthenticated sensitive endpoints (/api/signals, /api/sovereign, /api/harness/*, /api/observability, /api/cost-budget) where CORS is the only browser-side read barrier, so a page on a publicly-routable 100.x origin can read private trading/harness data cross-origin with no credentials (blocked by Chrome PNA at best; Firefox/Safari do not enforce PNA). P2 stands: real hardening defect with a genuine-but-conditioned leak path, one-line fix (tighten regex to 100.64.0.0/10 and share the predicate with the manual echo).

confirmed.title: SecretStr slack_bot_token passed to WebClient unwrapped in signals_server publish
confirmed.claim: `slack_token` is read as a pydantic `SecretStr` via `getattr(self.settings, "slack_bot_token", "")` and passed directly to `WebClient(token=slack_token)` without `.get_secret_value()`. When slack_sdk stringifies the token into the `Authorization: Bearer` header it becomes the masked `**********` (or raises on the non-str type), so `publish_signal` Slack posting fails authentication every time a token IS configured — a silent secret-unwrap defect. The truthiness guard `if not slack_token` happens to work only because this pydantic build gives SecretStr a `__len__`, masking the missing unwrap. Every other Anthropic/OpenAI call site unwraps (e.g. call_transcript_gpr.py:91-95).
confirmed.evidence: signals_server.py:457 — `slack_token = getattr(self.settings, "slack_bot_token", "") if self.settings else ""`; :459 `if not slack_token or not slack_channel_cfg:`; :477 `client = WebClient(token=slack_token)` (no `.get_secret_value()`). Contrast call_transcript_gpr.py:91 `if hasattr(anthropic_key, "get_secret_value"):` then `anthropic_key = anthropic_key.get_secret_value()`.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T5 'SecretStr unwrap discipline (project memory: SecretStr truthiness bug killed 4 overlays)'; role charter names SecretStr patterns explicitly. settings.py:574 `slack_bot_token: SecretStr`.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Unwrap before use: `from backend.agents.llm_client import unwrap_secret; slack_token = unwrap_secret(getattr(self.settings, 'slack_bot_token', ''))` (or `.get_secret_value()` with the hasattr guard), then keep the `if not slack_token` check on the plain string. Add a regression test asserting the header carries the real token.
confirmed.verify_reason: Confirmed. self.settings = get_settings() (signals_server.py:100) is the pydantic Settings with slack_bot_token: SecretStr (settings.py:574). Line 457 reads the SecretStr, line 477 passes it to WebClient(token=slack_token) with no .get_secret_value(). Installed slack_sdk base_client.py:59 calls token.strip(); SecretStr has no .strip() (verified in project venv), so WebClient construction raises AttributeError, swallowed by the generic except at :501 -> slack_reason='slack_api_error:AttributeError', slack_posted=False. Slack posting therefore fails silently EVERY time a token is configured; the empty-token guard at :459 passes because non-empty SecretStr is truthy via __len__, exactly as claimed. All other call sites (slack_bot/app.py:29, response_delivery.py:100, stuck_task_reaper.py:128, alerting.py:149, queue_notification.py:117) unwrap correctly. Not fixed, not in any prior-phase register (66.1/62.2 entries cover the correctly-unwrapped alerting.py path only). Minor mechanism drift: failure is AttributeError at construction, not a masked Bearer header, but impact is identical. P2 honest: trade booking precedes the Slack step and is unaffected; this is a silently-broken notification leg, no secret leak.

confirmed.title: Sole log-rotation + host-health authority (away-watchdog) is dead; backend.log at 84MB past its 50MB threshold, all other service logs have no rotation at all
confirmed.claim: The only log-rotation logic in the entire ops surface lives inside the away-ops healthcheck, whose launchd agent (com.pyfinagent.away-watchdog, StartInterval=1800, RunAtLoad=true) has runs=0 and has produced no health.jsonl line since 2026-07-06T23:03Z on a host up 52 days. backend.log is 84,276,482 bytes (threshold 52,428,800) and growing; the same outage silenced the kill-switch monitor, auth probe, disk check, and frontend-restart authority. frontend.log (15.4MB), handoff/logs/slack_bot.log (4.3MB), handoff/logs/auto-push.log (624KB) and handoff/autoresearch.log have NO rotation authority anywhere: no newsyslog.d entry, no RotatingFileHandler in backend/ (0 grep hits), launchd does not rotate StandardOutPath.
confirmed.evidence:
healthcheck.sh:246-252:
  log_rotated=false
  BLOG="$REPO/backend.log"
  if [ -f "$BLOG" ] && [ "$(stat -f%z "$BLOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)" -gt 52428800 ]; then
      rts=$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)
      if cp "$BLOG" "$REPO/handoff/logs/backend.log.$rts" ...
Empirical: `launchctl print gui/501/com.pyfinagent.away-watchdog` -> "state = not running / runs = 0 / last exit code = (never exited) / run interval = 1800"; tail health.jsonl last ts "2026-07-06T23:03:21Z"; `ls -la backend.log` -> 84276482 bytes 19 jul; uptime "up 52 days"; `ls /etc/newsyslog.d/` has no pyfinagent entry; `grep -rn RotatingFileHandler backend/` -> 0 hits.
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md A7 charter row ("log rotation") + S7 https://www.structlog.org/en/stable/logging-best-practices.html (log to stdout, rotation is the platform's job -- the platform half is absent here). Plist mtime 8 jul 10:38 postdates the last run: edited without bootout/bootstrap re-registration is the likely mechanism.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Move rotation to an always-on authority independent of away mode: a newsyslog.d(5) conf (or small dedicated launchd rotation agent) covering backend.log, frontend.log, handoff/logs/slack_bot.log, auto-push.log with size caps + compression, keeping the cp+truncate technique (launchd FDs are O_APPEND). Re-bootstrap com.pyfinagent.away-watchdog (bootout+bootstrap) and add a watchdog-liveness alarm (page when health.jsonl mtime exceeds 2h while the agent is loaded) so the watchdog itself is watched.
confirmed.verify_reason: Every empirical pillar re-verified live: healthcheck.sh:246-255 is the sole rotation authority in the repo (threshold 52428800, cp+truncate+gzip; no newsyslog.d pyfinagent entry, 0 RotatingFileHandler hits in backend/, launchd plists set StandardOutPath without rotation); launchctl print gui/501/com.pyfinagent.away-watchdog shows state=not running, runs=0, last exit=(never exited), StartInterval=1800, RunAtLoad=true; health.jsonl last line 2026-07-06T23:03:21Z on a host up 52 days; backend.log now 84,413,847 bytes (grown since the finding) with frontend.log 15.4MB and slack_bot.log 4.3MB unrotated. No guard neutralizes it: healthcheck.sh has no arming gate (meant to run every 30 min unconditionally per its header and contract_62.5.md), and the only other caller (PM away sessions per prompt_pm.md) also stopped producing artifacts 10 jul. contract_62.5/66.4 confirm the dead agent also owned kill-switch observation, auth-probe P1 paging, disk check, and frontend restart — all silenced with no meta-alarm. Not a duplicate: defect_register.md, harness_proposals.json, confirmed_findings.json, active_goal.md, and the phase-69 register contain no log-rotation or dead-watchdog item (all 'rotation' hits are sector/strategy rotation). Plist mtime 8 jul 10:38 postdating the last run supports the edit-without-rebootstrap mechanism. P1 is honest: not P0 (119GB disk free, no live trading-correctness breach), but a silently dead host-health/paging safety net plus unbounded log growth is latent-serious, not mere debt.

confirmed.title: start_services.sh pkill -9 races launchd KeepAlive into an unsupervised zombie backend; kill pattern does not match the frontend it starts; truncates the launchd-owned log
confirmed.claim: One manual run reproduces the project's zombie-worker class: (1) `pkill -9 uvicorn` SIGKILLs EVERY uvicorn on the machine with no graceful shutdown (in-flight paper-trading/BQ writes lost) including the launchd-supervised backend; com.pyfinagent.backend has KeepAlive=true + ThrottleInterval=5, so launchd respawns ~5s later while the script's nohup uvicorn (started after `sleep 2`) already holds :8000 -- the launchd twin then crash-loops on bind failure every 5s indefinitely, and the SERVING backend is an unsupervised nohup zombie that backend_watchdog.sh's `launchctl kickstart -k` cannot restart. (2) `pkill -9 "next dev"` never matches the `npx next start` process line 23 launches, so re-runs collide on :3000. (3) `> backend.log` O_TRUNC-clobbers the same file the launchd backend appends to.
confirmed.evidence:
start_services.sh:10-18,23:
  # Kill any existing instances
  pkill -9 uvicorn 2>/dev/null || true
  pkill -9 "next dev" 2>/dev/null || true
  sleep 2
  ...
  nohup python -m uvicorn backend.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > backend.log 2>&1 &
  ...
  nohup npx next start -p 3000 > ../frontend.log 2>&1 &
~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pyfinagent.backend.plist: <key>KeepAlive</key><true/> ... <key>ThrottleInterval</key><integer>5</integer> ... <key>StandardOutPath</key><string>.../backend.log</string>
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md A7 charter ("zombie-worker rule vs code", pkill footguns) + S2 https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/deployment/server-workers/ (exactly one supervisor owns the server process). Dedupe: phase-69 register item at backend/slack_bot/commands.py:295 is the Slack-command pkill surface; this script surface has 0 register hits.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the pkill+nohup path with `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$UID/com.pyfinagent.{backend,frontend}` when the agents exist (the slack_bot_monitor.sh phase-62.1 pattern already does this); keep a legacy path only behind an explicit flag, scoped `pkill -f "uvicorn backend.main"` with SIGTERM-then-wait, and never redirect onto the launchd-owned log file.
confirmed.verify_reason: Evidence verbatim at lines 10-23 and the launchd agent com.pyfinagent.backend is loaded/running with KeepAlive=true, ThrottleInterval=5, StandardOutPath=backend.log — but the P1 headline mechanism is empirically refuted: macOS pkill without -f matches comm, and the launchd uvicorn's comm is "Python" (shebang script; lsof shows listener COMMAND=Python), verified by `pgrep uvicorn` returning nothing while the backend runs. So `pkill -9 uvicorn` is a no-op and can never SIGKILL the launchd backend or trigger the respawn/bind crash-loop zombie race; a manual run today spawns a nohup uvicorn that dies on EADDRINUSE, then falsely reports healthy because the launchd backend answers the curl. Claims (2) and (3) are real: "next dev" never matches the `npx next start` it launches (rerun :3000 collision with the launchd frontend agent), and `> backend.log` O_TRUNC-clobbers the launchd-owned log (history loss/offset corruption). Additionally the script has no active caller (header's "Gateway Watchdog cron" is absent from crontab; no plist references it), making this a latent manual footgun. Not a duplicate — the phase-69.1 pkill item was slack_bot/commands.py:295, a different surface already removed. Real, in-context, worth fixing with the kickstart pattern backend_watchdog.sh:76 already uses, but downgraded to P2 debt.

confirmed.title: Nightly ablation launchd job has crash-failed silently for ~37 nights: raw `. backend/.env` sourcing hits the documented unbalanced-quote syntax error that phase-62.6 fixed only in run_nightly.sh
confirmed.claim: com.pyfinagent.ablation.plist runs `bash -c "cd ... && set -a && . backend/.env && ..."`; backend/.env is DOTENV format with an unbalanced quote at line 81, so the sourced shell dies with a syntax error before python ever runs -- 37 "unexpected EOF" occurrences in handoff/ablation.launchd.log (nightly since ~13 jun), launchctl last exit status 1, handoff/ablation.log frozen at 12 jun. run_nightly.sh was given a sanitized-grep sourcing fix for EXACTLY this failure (phase-62.6 comment) but the sibling ablation plist kept the raw source. No paging seam exists for nightly launchd job failures (autoresearch also exited FAIL rc=1 last night on an arxiv 429, unpaged).
confirmed.evidence:
run_nightly.sh:14-21 (the fix the ablation plist lacks):
  # phase-62.6 fix: the file is DOTENV format, not shell -- a wrapped comment
  # line with an unbalanced quote (introduced by an operator paste 2026-06-12)
  # made a raw `. .env` die with "unexpected EOF". Source a SANITIZED stream
  ...
  grep -E '^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*=' "$REPO/backend/.env" > "$_envtmp" 2>/dev/null || true
~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pyfinagent.ablation.plist ProgramArguments: <string>cd ... && set -a && . backend/.env && set +a && . .venv/bin/activate && python scripts/ablation/run_ablation.py --next-untested >> handoff/ablation.log 2>&1</string>
handoff/ablation.launchd.log (37 hits): backend/.env: line 81: unexpected EOF while looking for matching `"'
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md A7 charter (".env handling in scripts", silent failure paths) + the repo's own phase-62.6 fix rationale quoted above. Full-env sourcing also leaks every secret into the job's environment, vs backend_watchdog.sh:60's deliberate grep-only extraction.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Point the ablation plist at a wrapper script using run_nightly.sh's sanitized-sourcing block (also fixes the secret-env exposure); repair backend/.env line 81; add a cheap failure seam (wrapper logs FAIL rc + optional bot-token page after N consecutive failures) for both nightly jobs.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified end-to-end: com.pyfinagent.ablation.plist raw-sources backend/.env in bash -c while run_nightly.sh:14-27 carries the phase-62.6 sanitized-grep fix for exactly this DOTENV unbalanced-quote failure; ablation.launchd.log has exactly 37 'line 81: unexpected EOF' hits, launchctl list shows last exit status 1 for the ablation job (and autoresearch), and ablation.log mtime is frozen at Jun 12 03:00 2026. No wrapper/guard neutralizes it and no phase-69/73 register item records it (harness_proposals.json line 215 even mislabels the job as active/healthy). Mitigating nuance: last successful run printed 'all-features-tested' and run_ablation.py --next-untested exits 0 as a no-op on an empty queue, so no ablation work is currently lost — which caps this at P2 debt (broken job, corrupted .env, raw secret sourcing, no failure paging seam) rather than P1.

confirmed.title: PreToolUse danger guard has no pkill/killall pattern despite the project's proven kill-vector class and daily --dangerously-skip-permissions sessions
confirmed.claim: The guard blocks rm -rf, force-push variants, git reset --hard, file-level checkout/restore, backend/.env writes, execute_sql, and launchctl removal verbs on pyfinagent agents -- but a Bash tool call `pkill -9 -f python` or `pkill -9 uvicorn` (which SIGKILLs the live trading backend, autonomous loop, Slack bot, and the harness itself) passes unguarded. Phase-69 confirmed this exact kill class as critical on the Slack-command surface (commands.py:295 "clear queue" -> pkill -9 -f python); the hook layer -- the designated away-ops defense-in-depth seam, active under daily unattended `--dangerously-skip-permissions` sessions (run_away_session.sh:161) -- leaves the same class open.
confirmed.evidence:
.claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-danger.sh:4-8 (the whole block list):
  # Blocks the following unless explicitly allowed:
  #   - rm -rf ...
  #   - git push --force / --force-with-lease / -f
  #   - git reset --hard ...
  #   - mcp__<server>__execute_sql  (unless MIGRATE token in env)
:162-163 (launchctl rail, the analogous pattern that exists):
  if re.search(r"(^|\s)launchctl\s+(bootout|unload|remove|disable)\s", seg) and "com.pyfinagent." in seg:
      print("block:launchctl removal on pyfinagent agent"); sys.exit(0)
`grep -n pkill .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-danger.sh` -> 0 hits.
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T8 Agent Audit (arXiv:2603.22853): "hook configs are AUDIT SURFACE"; boundary-aware elevated scrutiny at agent boundaries. Dedupe: phase-69 register line 65 covers only the backend/slack_bot/commands.py surface; handoff/current/harness_proposals.json has no pkill/guard item (checked 2026-07-19).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add a segment-scoped rail mirroring the launchctl one: block `pkill|killall` whose target matches python|uvicorn|next|slack_bot (and any `kill -9` of a pgrep result piped from those), with the same CLAUDE_ALLOW_DANGER escape hatch; message points to `launchctl kickstart -k` as the sanctioned restart path.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified: hook has zero pkill/killall patterns (grep confirmed) while blocking rm -rf, force-push, git reset --hard, checkout/restore, backend/.env writes, execute_sql, and launchctl removal verbs (lines 162-163 verbatim as quoted). Gap is real in BOTH defense layers: the settings.json deny list (lines 179-189, enforced even under bypassPermissions) mirrors the hook's block set and also omits pkill/killall. run_away_session.sh:161 confirmed running --dangerously-skip-permissions, making the hook the load-bearing rail. Not a duplicate: phase-69 register line 65 covers only the commands.py:295 Slack surface (sink removed in 69.1 per live_check_69.1.md); harness_proposals.json has no pkill guard item. Not intentional: the launchctl rail's "kickstart is the allowed restart path" message shows process-lifecycle ops are deliberately railed — pkill is an omission from the same class. P2 honest: trigger requires the agent itself to issue pkill (low probability, unlike the substring-triggered Slack sink), so this is defense-in-depth debt with severe consequence-if-triggered, not a live P0/P1 defect.

confirmed.title: Live Slack bot token hardcoded in crontab and long-lived OAuth token in world-readable (644) launchd plists -- direct drift from the project's own secrets rule
confirmed.claim: The active crontab entry inlines the production Slack bot token (`export SLACK_BOT_TOKEN='xoxb-100376...' && .../slack_mention_checker.sh`) instead of using the script's own openclaw.json fallback (lines 27-29) or backend/.env grep pattern used everywhere else. Separately, com.pyfinagent.away-session-{am,pm}.plist and away-watchdog.plist are mode -rw-r--r-- (644) and embed the 1-year CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN in plaintext, readable by any local process/user; frontend.plist embeds AUTH_SECRET + AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET (at least mode 600). .claude/rules/security.md mandates "never hardcode secrets".
confirmed.evidence:
crontab -l (token masked):
  */2 * * * * export SLACK_BOT_TOKEN='xoxb-10037646482576-...' && /Users/ford/.openclaw/workspace/pyfinagent/scripts/slack_mention_checker.sh >> /tmp/slack_mention_checker.log 2>&1
slack_mention_checker.sh:27-29 (the fallback the cron line bypasses):
  if [ -z "$SLACK_BOT_TOKEN" ]; then
      SLACK_BOT_TOKEN=$(cat ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.channels.slack.botToken' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
`ls -la ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pyfinagent.away-session-am.plist` -> -rw-r--r-- 1 ford staff 1461 8 jul. 10:38 (contains <key>CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN</key> plaintext).
confirmed.basis: .claude/rules/security.md "Secret Management ... never hardcode secrets" + handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T5 https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Secrets_Management_Cheat_Sheet.html (env vars "generally accessible to all processes and may be included in logs or system dumps"; the repo's away-week 2,101-plaintext-key incident proves the class is live here).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Drop the inline export from crontab (let the script's openclaw.json fallback or a 600-mode env file supply it) and rotate the exposed xoxb token; chmod 600 all com.pyfinagent.*.plist; longer-term move plist secrets to `sh -c '. /path/600file && exec ...'` indirection so plists carry no credential bytes.
confirmed.verify_reason: All three evidence legs verified live: (1) crontab -l inlines the production xoxb Slack bot token via `export SLACK_BOT_TOKEN='xoxb-10037646482576-...' &&`, which also leaks the token into ps-visible command lines every 2 minutes when cron runs it through sh -c; (2) scripts/slack_mention_checker.sh:27-29 contains the openclaw.json fallback exactly as quoted, making the inline export unnecessary; (3) com.pyfinagent.away-session-am/pm.plist and away-watchdog.plist are all mode 644 (-rw-r--r--) and each embeds CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (sk-ant-oat01-...) in plaintext, while sibling backend.plist/frontend.plist are already 600 — proving the 644 files are drift, not convention. .claude/rules/security.md:26 mandates never hardcoding secrets. No wrapper/guard neutralizes any of this. Searched confirmed_findings.json, audit_phase69/{confirmed,contested,refuted,register}.json/md, harness_proposals.json, and design_pack_73 — no prior register item covers the crontab token or the 644 plists (the only plist-adjacent entry is the unrelated PAPER_MAX env-shadow finding). P2 is honest: real local secrets exposure with trivial remediation on a single-user dev machine, not a live remote compromise (P0/P1) and more than polish (P3).

confirmed.title: Unattended wrappers make unbounded network calls: git pull holds the away-session lock forever on hang; cron Slack poll has no curl timeout
confirmed.claim: run_away_session.sh wraps the `claude` launch in gtimeout (line 160) but runs `git pull --rebase origin main` bare -- a half-open connection to GitHub hangs the wrapper indefinitely WHILE HOLDING handoff/.away-session.lock, and because the stale-lock reaper only reaps DEAD holders (ps liveness + name check passes for the hung process), every subsequent scheduled session logs SKIP forever until manual kill -- in an unattended context designed to survive weeks alone. Same class: slack_mention_checker.sh:38 curl to slack.com has no -m (cron every 2 min; its lock expires at 120s, so hung curls accumulate), and mas_harness/run_cycle.sh:60 runs `claude -p` with no timeout at all (latent; agent currently unloaded).
confirmed.evidence:
run_away_session.sh:107 vs 160:
  if ! git pull --rebase origin main >> "$SLOG" 2>&1; then
  ...
  "$GTIMEOUT" -k 60 "$CAP" "$CLAUDE_BIN" -p \
:49 (the reaper that spares a HUNG holder):
  if [ -n "$holder" ] && ps -p "$holder" -o command= 2>/dev/null | grep -q "run_away_session"; then
      return 1  # live session holds it
slack_mention_checker.sh:38:
  RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST https://slack.com/api/conversations.history \
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md A7 charter ("missing timeouts on network calls") + S9 precedent (googleapis/python-bigquery#2310 hang-on-network-drop class; the repo's 30s-timeout convention exists for exactly this). Contrast: every OTHER network call in these scripts carries -m/timeout (healthcheck.sh curls -m 10, sentinel urllib timeout=8, metered_spend .result(timeout=25)).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Wrap the pull: `"$GTIMEOUT" -k 10 120 git pull --rebase origin main` (falling into the existing offline-mode branch on rc=124); add `-m 15` to the slack_mention_checker curl; gtimeout-wrap run_cycle.sh's claude call before any re-enable of the mas-harness agent.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in code: run_away_session.sh:107 runs bare `git pull --rebase origin main` after acquiring handoff/.away-session.lock (line 58), while only the claude launch (line 160) is gtimeout-wrapped; the stale-lock reaper (lines 49-50) spares any live PID matching 'run_away_session', so a wrapper hung on the pull holds the lock and every later session SKIPs. No neutralizer: git config has no http.lowSpeedLimit/timeout, and the loaded away-watchdog (healthcheck.sh) only monitors backend/frontend/slack-bot -- it never kills hung sessions or reaps the lock. away-session-am/pm are loaded in launchctl, so the path is live. Secondary legs confirmed: scripts/slack_mention_checker.sh:38 curl has no -m and runs from live crontab every 2 min with a 120s-stale lock (hung curls accumulate); mas_harness/run_cycle.sh:60 claude has no timeout but the mas-harness plist is disabled (.bak, not loaded) -- latent as claimed. Not a duplicate of any phase-69/70/71/72/73 register item. Minor drift only: slack_mention_checker.sh lives in scripts/, not scripts/away_ops/. P2 honest -- rare trigger, unattended-availability debt, not live correctness.

confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md A7 charter ("shell scripts without set -euo pipefail", "silent failure paths") + T5 OWASP secrets (hardcoded/uploaded secrets) + T5 API8 security misconfiguration (--allow-unauthenticated). Streamlit-era references (pyfinagent-app/.streamlit/secrets.toml, line 121) date it as pre-FastAPI legacy.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Delete the script (git history preserves it), or if any Cloud Function is still wanted: add `set -euo pipefail`, `cd <dir> || exit 1` guards, a .gcloudignore, and drop --allow-unauthenticated in favor of IAM-gated invocation.
confirmed.verify_reason: Core defect verified: deploy_agents.sh has zero strict mode (grep 'set -e' = 0 hits), and all four cd targets (quant-agent, ingestion_agent, pyfinagent-app, earnings-ingestion-agent) are absent from the repo (ls confirms x4). Each unguarded `cd` failure leaves the shell in the current directory, `git diff --quiet HEAD -- .` then evaluates that directory (repo is dirty: 9 files in git status --porcelain), and the mismatched `cd ..` after each block walks the cwd upward, so a run would issue `gcloud functions deploy --source=. --allow-unauthenticated` from the repo root (or even its parent) against the still-configured live project sunny-might-477607-p8, after mutating IAM bindings at lines 72-111. Not a duplicate: no deploy_agents/sre-ops hits in confirmed_findings.json, audit_phase69, or harness_proposals.json. However, the headline consequence is overstated on three counts: (1) backend/.env and both .env.bak files are gitignored and untracked, and with a .gitignore present gcloud's default gcloudignore includes #!include:.gitignore, so the .env secrets would NOT be in the source upload despite no .gcloudignore file; (2) repo root has no main.py, so the buildpack build for entry-point quant_agent would fail server-side and no public function would actually materialize -- exposure is limited to non-ignored repo source landing in the project's private build-staging bucket; (3) two pre-deploy guards (Redis describe empty -> exit 1 at line 92; missing secrets.toml -> exit 1 at line 129 if the Secret Manager entry is gone) may abort before line 142. The script is still a real hazard-if-run legacy artifact worth deleting (IAM mutation side effects, --allow-unauthenticated pattern, live project ID), so P2 debt severity is honest; the P0/P1-flavored 'public .env exposure' narrative in the claim should not be repeated in the register.

confirmed.title: Frontend has two conflicting serving authorities: launchd KeepAlive runs `next dev` (dev server) as the permanent service while start_services.sh runs `next start`; the launchd agent is currently not running at all
confirmed.claim: com.pyfinagent.frontend.plist ProgramArguments is `next dev --port 3000` under KeepAlive=true -- a development server (HMR file watchers, uncompiled on-demand builds, higher memory, no production optimizations) as the always-on serving process on the trading host -- while start_services.sh serves the same port with production `next start` and kills only "next dev" (the mismatch behind sre-ops-02 point 2). Live state: `launchctl print` shows the frontend agent state=not running (runs=5) despite KeepAlive, and frontend.log's last write is 17 jul -- the service is currently dead with two half-authorities and no clear owner.
confirmed.evidence:
~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pyfinagent.frontend.plist:
  <string>/Users/ford/.openclaw/workspace/pyfinagent/frontend/node_modules/.bin/next</string>
  <string>dev</string>
  <string>--port</string>
  <string>3000</string>
  ... <key>KeepAlive</key><true/>
start_services.sh:12,23:
  pkill -9 "next dev" 2>/dev/null || true
  nohup npx next start -p 3000 > ../frontend.log 2>&1 &
`launchctl print gui/501/com.pyfinagent.frontend` -> "state = not running / runs = 5 / last exit code = 0"; frontend.log mtime 17 jul 21:20.
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md T3 canonical (Next.js docs: `next dev` is development mode; production serving is `next build` + `next start`) + S2 deployment-guidance analog (one supervisor, production server in production). No register hits for frontend/launchd (phase-69 grep = 0).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Pick launchd as the single authority: plist runs `next start -p 3000` against a committed build (build step in a pre-start wrapper or on deploy), re-bootstrap the agent; reduce start_services.sh to `launchctl kickstart -k` calls (per sre-ops-02).
confirmed.verify_reason: All quoted evidence verified exactly: plist runs `next dev --port 3000` with KeepAlive=true; start_services.sh:12 pkills "next dev" and :23 starts `npx next start -p 3000`; launchctl shows state=not running, runs=5, last exit code=0; frontend.log mtime 17 jul 21:20. One correction: the frontend is not dead — port 3000 is live via a THIRD unmanaged authority, an orphaned manual `npm run dev` (PID 20615, PPID 1, started 17 jul 21:20, matching the log mtime) running next-server v15.5.12 in dev mode. This strengthens the no-clear-owner claim: three serving mechanisms exist and the currently live one is owned by neither documented authority, with output unlogged. No duplicate in phase-69/70/71/72/73 registers (grep of audit_phase69/register.md, confirmed.json, confirmed_findings.json = zero launchd/start_services hits); sre-ops-02 is a distinct sibling finding in the same run (supervisor duplication), cross-referenced not duplicated. P2 honest: ops/config debt with real reliability hazard on the trading host, but no live correctness/security defect in scope.

confirmed.title: mas_harness/run_cycle.sh keeps the non-atomic lock + PID-recycling liveness check that run_away_session.sh was explicitly hardened against
confirmed.claim: The lock is check-then-create (two racers can both pass line 23 before line 32 writes) and staleness uses bare `kill -0 $lock_pid`, which succeeds for ANY process that recycled the PID -- a stale lock then blocks every future cycle with SKIP until that unrelated process exits. run_away_session.sh:12-13 documents this exact weakness ("check-then-create is not atomic; PIDs get recycled") and fixed it with noclobber + process-name verification, but the fix was never backported. Latent: the mas-harness launchd agent is currently disabled (disabled.com.pyfinagent.mas-harness.plist.bak), so severity is capped until re-enable.
confirmed.evidence:
run_cycle.sh:23-32:
  if [ -f "$LOCKFILE" ]; then
      lock_pid=$(cat "$LOCKFILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
      if [ -n "$lock_pid" ] && kill -0 "$lock_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
          echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] SKIP previous cycle still running (pid=$lock_pid)" >> "$LOGFILE"
          exit 0
      fi
      rm -f "$LOCKFILE"
  fi
  echo $$ > "$LOCKFILE"
run_away_session.sh:12-13: "Lockfile hardened vs run_cycle.sh: noclobber atomic create + PID liveness + process-name check (check-then-create is not atomic; PIDs get recycled)."
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md A7 charter (process-management footguns) + the repo's own hardened reference implementation at scripts/away_ops/run_away_session.sh:41-56.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Backport the away wrapper's acquire_lock() (noclobber atomic create + `ps -p $holder -o command=` name check) before any re-enable of the mas-harness agent; add a gtimeout cap on the claude call at the same time (sre-ops-07).
confirmed.verify_reason: Evidence verbatim at run_cycle.sh:23-32: non-atomic check-then-create lock plus bare `kill -0` staleness (PID recycling false-positive). run_away_session.sh:12-13/41-56 confirms the repo's own hardened fix (noclobber + ps -o command= name check) was never backported. Latency confirmed and slightly stronger than claimed: launchctl shows no mas-harness agent loaded (only disabled.*.bak plists), AND phase-71.6 (harness_log:27504) classified run_cycle.sh a neutralized dead driver kept only as a live test fixture for 3 consumers (revert_hygiene_drill.py, smoke_test_4_17_11.py, test_meta_cron.py). Not a duplicate: no run_cycle lock item in phase-69 register, confirmed_findings.json, or harness_proposals.json; the 71.6 KEPT-WITH-REASON decision covered deletion, not the lock defect. P3 honest for latent-behind-re-enable. Fixer caveat: revert_hygiene_drill.py asserts hardened-file markers in run_cycle.sh, so the backport must keep that drill green.

confirmed.title: Lock-count drift guard fails today (16 real hits vs EXPECTED_LOCK_COUNT=15) and is CI-ignored, so a threading.Lock was added without the mandated re-entrancy re-audit
confirmed.claim: Replicating the test's exact counting logic (regex threading\.Lock\s*\(\s*\), non-comment lines, tests/ and __pycache__ excluded) yields 16 hits — e.g. backend/agents/claude_code_client.py:102 `_RAIL_GUARD_LOCK = threading.Lock()` and backend/api/job_status_api.py:87 `_lock = threading.Lock()` postdate the phase-56.2 roster — vs the pinned 15. The test therefore fails when run, meaning (a) at least one lock entered the codebase without the re-entrancy re-audit the test exists to force (the kill_switch snapshot() deadlock it documents proves this bug class is live here), and (b) nobody noticed because the file is on the e2e-smoke --ignore list and the full local suite (aggregate.sh criterion 'pytest_backend_tests_passes_with_zero_failures') has evidently not been run since the lock landed.

test_phase_23_2_14_no_reentrant_locks.py:24 `EXPECTED_LOCK_COUNT = 15`; :63-66 `    assert count == EXPECTED_LOCK_COUNT, (
        f"phase-23.2.14 roster drift: found {count} threading.Lock() instances "

"`; grep replication (literal-paren, non-comment, non-test) = 16 sites incl. `backend/agents/claude_code_client.py:102:_RAIL_GUARD_LOCK = threading.Lock()` and `backend/api/job_status_api.py:87:_lock = threading.Lock()`; e2e-smoke.yml:75 `            --ignore=backend/tests/test_phase_23_2_14_no_reentrant_locks.py \`.

Git history confirms the unaudited lock is _RAIL_GUARD_LOCK (backend/agents/claude_code_client.py:102), added in phase-66.1 commit 27d40df5, after the phase-56.2 re-audit pinned the count at 15. One evidence correction: job_status_api.py:87 does NOT postdate phase-56.2 (it dates to phase-10.5 commit 1122a021 and was in the audited roster); the drift is exactly one lock, not two. Not registered in handoff/current (the phase-53.3 note covered the earlier 14->15 drift, resolved in 56.2). P2 is honest: deterministic guard silently red + one lock without the mandated re-entrancy audit is real debt, but no live deadlock demonstrated.

confirmed.title: Seed-Stability 'Gate' re-validates a frozen 2026-04-16 JSON on every PR — it recomputes statistics over committed data and can never catch a code regression
confirmed.claim: The workflow claims 'A failing drill blocks the PR' as a reproducibility gate, but the drill (scripts/go_live_drills/seed_stability_test.py) is stdlib-only: it reads handoff/seed_stability_results.json (last written 2026-04-16, git 44c44098) and recomputes mean/std over those stored Sharpe values. No backtest is executed, so the gate passes identically on every PR regardless of what the code does — it can only fail if someone edits the committed JSON. Three months of backtest-engine and strategy changes have never re-exercised the 5-seed run; this is a golden-file check whose 'current output' side was never wired in.
confirmed.evidence:
seed-stability-check.yml:4-8 `# `scripts/go_live_drills/seed_stability_test.py` reads
# `handoff/seed_stability_results.json` and asserts that the
# Sharpe stddev across 5 random seeds is below 0.1 ... A failing drill
# blocks the PR.`; seed_stability_test.py:3-5 `Stdlib-only drill. Verifies that the seed stability test has been run
with 5 different random seeds and that the resulting Sharpe values
have std < 0.1`; drill main(): `data = json.loads(SEED_RESULTS_PATH.read_text())` then `check("S5 std Sharpe < 0.1 (checklist gate)", std_sharpe < STD_THRESHOLD, ...)`; `git log --oneline -- handoff/seed_stability_results.json` = one commit `44c44098 Phase 4.4.1.3: seed stability test -- FAIL (seed-stable but Sharpe < 0.9)`, file mtime 16 apr.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T7 rule 4 golden-file guidance ('persist a validated output; compare CURRENT test results against previously validated solutions' — the current-results half is absent) + T8 rule 4.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Either have the workflow regenerate results (invoke scripts/harness/run_seed_stability.py on a pinned fixture window before the drill) or rename/re-scope the lane to 'baseline-shape check' and drop the 'blocks the PR' reproducibility claim; also refresh the stale April baseline.
confirmed.verify_reason: Confirmed. The workflow's only gate step (line 33) runs scripts/go_live_drills/seed_stability_test.py, which is stdlib-only and never executes a backtest: line 77 reads the committed handoff/seed_stability_results.json and S1-S11 recompute mean/std over the stored Sharpe values (S8/S9 even compare the JSON's own stored mean/std against values recomputed from the same JSON). git log shows the JSON has exactly one commit (44c44098, 2026-04-16), so the 'reproducibility gate' passes identically on every PR and cannot detect any code regression — the 'current output' half of the golden-file pattern was never wired in. Aggravating detail: the original run was recorded as FAIL on Sharpe level, but the drill demotes Sharpe level to a non-gating soft note (SN1), so the frozen data green-lights all PRs. Not intentional: the drill docstring shows it was a one-time Phase 4.4.1.3 checklist verification ('verifies the test HAS BEEN RUN'), later relabeled by phase-25.B6 as an enforcing PR gate. scripts/harness/run_seed_stability.py exists, so the suggested regeneration fix is viable. No duplicate in phase-69/70/71/72/73 registers (confirmed_findings.json, harness_proposals.json, design_audit_burndown_69.md all clean of this item). P2 honest: CI-debt producing false go-live assurance, no direct live correctness/security impact.

confirmed.title: Visual-regression CI has zero baselines — every snapshot dir contains only .gitkeep since 2026-05-13, so the lane fails or is vacuous on every frontend push/PR
confirmed.claim: All 8 visual specs call toHaveScreenshot (maxDiffPixelRatio 0.015) but the committed snapshot tree holds only empty .gitkeep files — the documented operator first-run flow (workflow_dispatch with update_snapshots=true, auto-commit PNGs) was never executed in the 2+ months since phase-25.A12 (single commit 73c9ba39, dirs dated 13 May). On CI, Playwright fails when an expected snapshot is missing, so the push/PR-triggered `npx playwright test` step has never been able to pass: the repo carries a permanently-red (or ignored) visual lane that provides zero regression protection while claiming it, training alert fatigue on the workflow list.

confirmed.title: Root tests/ tree top level carries collection-breaking relics: sys.path points at a nonexistent tests/backend dir, and 4 async tests have no async plugin under installed pytest 9
confirmed.claim: tests/test_end_to_end.py, tests/test_queue_processor.py and tests/test_response_delivery.py insert `Path(__file__).parent / "backend"` — i.e. tests/backend, which does not exist — then import `db.tickets_db`/`services.*` (real modules live at backend/db/tickets_db.py), so they ImportError at collection under any invocation; additionally they are `async def` tests and the venv has pytest 9.0.3 with NO pytest-asyncio (anyio's plugin only handles @pytest.mark.anyio), so even with imports fixed pytest>=8.4 fails un-plugged async tests, and tests/test_mcp_integration.py:49 relies on an unregistered pytest.mark.asyncio. Net: `pytest tests/` cannot complete collection, while runbooks (docs/runbooks/meta_evolution_rollback.md:73) and masterplan verification commands depend on subdir-scoped runs of this same tree — the 94-file tree has no owner and its top level is dead weight that breaks whole-tree and bare-`pytest` collection.
confirmed.evidence: tests/test_end_to_end.py:13-15 `# Add backend to path\nbackend_path = Path(__file__).parent / "backend"\nsys.path.insert(0, str(backend_path))` then :20-24 `from db.tickets_db import (...)` (module exists only at backend/db/tickets_db.py; tests/db/ contains only test_tickets_db_no_fd_leak.py); tests/test_queue_processor.py:17-24 identical pattern; async heads: tests/test_end_to_end.py:27 `async def test_end_to_end_ticket_system():`; venv listing: `pytest-9.0.3.dist-info`, `anyio-4.13.0.dist-info`, no pytest-asyncio.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T7 rule 7 ('Async tests need pytest-asyncio (NOT in requirements.txt — verify how backend/tests currently run coroutines)') + pytest goodpractices unique-layout guidance (https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/explanation/goodpractices.html).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Delete or repair the 4 top-level relics (fix sys.path to parents[1], convert to sync or add pytest-asyncio + asyncio_mode=auto), and register/replace the asyncio marker; then verify `pytest tests/` collects clean.
confirmed.verify_reason: Empirically reproduced: pytest 9.0.3 + no pytest-asyncio in .venv (only anyio/cov/timeout); tests/test_end_to_end.py:14 (and test_queue_processor.py:17, test_response_delivery.py:13) insert nonexistent tests/backend onto sys.path then import db.tickets_db/services.* which live under backend/; running `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only` yields 'Interrupted: 7 errors during collection' (ModuleNotFoundError: db.tickets_db) — the finding actually undercounts (7 files fail collection, also test_ingestion.py, test_deduplication.py, test_tickets_db.py, tests/services/test_persist_lite_analysis.py). PytestUnknownMarkWarning for pytest.mark.asyncio at test_mcp_integration.py:48 confirmed; pytest.ini registers only requires_live, no asyncio marker/mode; no conftest.py neutralizes anything. Not in any phase-69/73 register. P3 honest: runbooks use subdir-scoped runs that still pass collection, so blast radius is the dead top-level relics breaking whole-tree runs.

confirmed.title: pytest.ini is marker-only: no testpaths, no addopts/--strict-markers, no filterwarnings, and installed pytest-timeout is never configured
confirmed.claim: The root pytest.ini registers only the requires_live marker. Consequences: (a) bare `pytest` from repo root collects BOTH trees (127 + 94 files) including the qa-tests-08 collection breakers, so the canonical full-suite command is undocumented and un-runnable; (b) no --strict-markers, so the unregistered pytest.mark.asyncio (tests/test_mcp_integration.py) and any future typo'd requires_live silently degrade to warnings, defeating the quarantine; (c) no filterwarnings, so the Python 3.14 deprecation sweep the brief mandates (-W error::DeprecationWarning) has no home; (d) pytest-timeout 2.4.0 is installed in the venv but no timeout default is set anywhere, leaving the documented BQ hang-on-network-drop class able to hang a suite run; (e) duplicate basename test_observability.py exists in both trees (backend/tests/ and tests/api/), the exact prepend-import-mode collision pytest warns about if the trees are ever co-collected.
confirmed.evidence: pytest.ini:7-9 (entire config) `[pytest]\nmarkers =\n    requires_live: test depends on live BigQuery / live-system state; skipped unless PYFINAGENT_LIVE_TESTS=1`; venv listing shows `pytest_timeout-2.4.0.dist-info` with zero `timeout` config in pytest.ini/workflows; dupe check output: `test_observability.py` appears in both backend/tests/ and tests/api/; marker census shows `1 pytest.mark.asyncio` (unregistered).
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T7 rule 1 ('pytest.ini has NO testpaths, NO addopts, NO filterwarnings — auditors check discovery cost, warning hygiene ... importlib mode adoption') + T7 rule 7 (--strict-markers, pytest 8.x line current; https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/explanation/goodpractices.html) + T2 rule 6 (3.14 deprecation sweep).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Set `testpaths = backend/tests tests`, `addopts = --strict-markers --import-mode=importlib`, a filterwarnings baseline (error::DeprecationWarning with targeted ignores), `timeout = 120` for the requires_live lane, and register/purge the asyncio marker after qa-tests-08.
confirmed.verify_reason: pytest.ini (lines 7-9) is verifiably marker-only: no testpaths, addopts, filterwarnings, or timeout, and no other pytest config exists anywhere in the repo (no root/backend pyproject.toml, setup.cfg, or tox.ini). pytest-timeout 2.4.0 is installed in .venv with zero timeout config. pytest.mark.asyncio at tests/test_mcp_integration.py:48 is unregistered (pytest-asyncio not installed) and would silently warn without --strict-markers. CI runs only backend/tests/ with 6 --ignore flags, so bare `pytest` co-collects both trees uncontrolled. Sub-claim (e) is overstated: the duplicate test_observability.py basenames are both inside __init__.py packages (backend/__init__.py, backend/tests/__init__.py, tests/api/__init__.py), so prepend-mode module names are distinct and no collision fires — but this does not sink the finding. Not present in any phase-69/70/71/72/73 register. P3 (test-infra polish) is honest.

confirmed.title: Frontend vitest unit suite (10 component tests) runs in no CI lane; e2e-functional Playwright suite is env-gated to manual Mac runs only
confirmed.claim: frontend/src has 10+ vitest component tests (including money-display components PortfolioAllocationDonut, ComputeCostBreakdown, AlphaLeaderboard) wired to `npm run test` via scripts/run-test.mjs, but no workflow ever invokes vitest — the e2e-smoke frontend step runs only `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build`, and grep across all 10 workflows finds zero vitest/run-test/npm-test invocations. confirmed.title: Live CC rail silently drops schema enforcement: Pydantic response_schema never reaches --json-schema and no system prompt is ever passed

The rail that makes live decisions runs on prompt-coaxed JSON with zero schema enforcement and none of the _HOUSE_INSTRUCTIONS JSON/calibration/safety rules, while enforcement exists only on the dormant direct-API ClaudeClient path -- parse failures degrade silently to None/HOLD fallbacks.

confirmed.title: LLMResponse discards stop_reason, so the documented max_tokens/refusal structured-output caveat is unenforceable at every parse site (and no site re-validates with Pydantic)
confirmed.claim: Anthropic's structured-outputs doc (anchored in the research brief) says output may NOT match the schema when stop_reason is max_tokens or refusal and parse sites must check stop_reason before trusting JSON. ClaudeClient logs 'partial output' on max_tokens and returns the truncated text in an LLMResponse that has NO stop_reason field, and refusal becomes a prose sentinel string -- so orchestrator/debate/risk_debate parse sites physically cannot distinguish 'truncated by budget' (retry with bigger max_tokens would fix) from 'malformed' (retry won't), and all collapse into the same silent None/default fallback. Additionally, zero call sites Pydantic-re-validate parsed dicts (grep model_validate/parse_obj: no hits in agents/), the doc's required server-side re-validation companion.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T6 rule 2 verbatim: "stop_reason == 'max_tokens' / 'refusal' mean output may NOT match schema -- parse sites must check stop_reason before trusting JSON; ... server-side re-validation required" (https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/structured-outputs).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add stop_reason: Optional[str] to LLMResponse and populate it in ClaudeClient/GeminiClient (finish_reason)/ClaudeCodeClient (envelope stop_reason); at the shared parse helpers (_parse_json_with_fallback, debate/risk_debate _parse_json) branch: max_tokens -> single retry at higher budget or explicit degraded flag; then model_validate against the schema class before use.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified in code: LLMResponse (llm_client.py:653-672) has no stop_reason field; ClaudeClient's max_tokens-on-text branch (1683-1685) only logs and returns unmarked truncated text, and refusal (1690-1694) becomes a prose sentinel no consumer detects (only multi_agent_orchestrator.py:1417 forwards it verbatim). Grep confirms zero stop_reason/model_validate/parse_obj hits in orchestrator.py, debate.py, risk_debate.py; debate._parse_json (122-128) returns None on JSONDecodeError, so budget-truncated, refused, and malformed outputs are indistinguishable and all collapse to the same silent fallback. GeminiClient equally discards finish_reason (MAX_TOKENS None-text is coerced to "" in __post_init__). Not a duplicate: the only stop_reason register item (audit_phase69/contested.json) is the distinct double-thinking-kwarg TypeError in multi_agent_orchestrator's retry. P2 is honest: fallbacks prevent crashes, so this is robustness/observability debt, not live breakage.

confirmed.title: Per-call JSON schema is concatenated INSIDE the cache_control system block, fragmenting the 1h prompt cache and violating the module's own design constraint
confirmed.claim: The _HOUSE_INSTRUCTIONS header mandates 'DO NOT inline Pydantic JSON schemas' and 'Append dynamic content (schemas...) AFTER this prefix so the cached prefix stays stable', but generate_content appends the schema JSON to system_prompt and then puts the COMBINED text in a single block carrying cache_control -- the cache breakpoint therefore sits AFTER the schema, so every distinct schema mints its own ~4.5-5K-token cache entry at the 2.0x 1h-TTL write premium (cost_tracker.py:170-174: one 4096-token write = ~$0.041 on Opus) instead of one shared house-instructions entry; agents alternating schemas churn writes and depress the hit rate the 25.B9 padding was built to guarantee.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T6 rule 6 (token hygiene/audit drift) + Anthropic prompt-caching prefix semantics (cache_control marks the END of the cached prefix; write premium 2.0x at 1h TTL -- pricing mirrored at cost_tracker.py:166-174).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Split into two system blocks: [{text: _HOUSE_INSTRUCTIONS, cache_control: 1h}, {text: schema_hint}] so the stable prefix caches once and the schema rides uncached (or gets its own second breakpoint for hot schemas). Verify with cache_creation_input_tokens before/after on two alternating schemas.
confirmed.verify_reason: CONFIRMED. llm_client.py:1360-1368 appends the per-call Pydantic schema JSON onto system_prompt (= _HOUSE_INSTRUCTIONS) and lines 1383-1389 put the COMBINED string in one system block with cache_control {ephemeral, ttl:1h}; the breakpoint therefore sits AFTER the schema, so each distinct schema mints its own ~5.4K-token cache entry at the 2.0x 1h write premium (cost_tracker.py:166-175 confirms 2.0x and the ~$0.041/4096-tok Opus write). This directly violates the module's own design constraints at lines 63-65 and the inline comment at 1354-1359, which falsely claims the schema rides 'AFTER the cached prefix'. Phase-25.B9 artifacts (evaluator_critique.md, experiment_results.md) mis-certified this exact behavior as correct, and no phase-69/73 register item flags it (the phase-71 mas-cache REJECT item concerns multi_agent_orchestrator.py and cites this code as the correct reference pattern), so it is neither fixed nor queued. One tempering caveat: alternating schemas do not evict each other (each prefix caches independently and hits on reuse within TTL), so the hit-rate-churn portion of the claim is overstated — the real waste is one duplicated 2x-premium ~5K-token write per distinct schema per TTL window plus the design-contract violation. Cost/efficiency debt with a trivial two-block fix, no correctness impact: P2 is honest.

confirmed.title: cost_tracker double-subtracts cache tokens from input_tokens, zeroing the uncached-input cost on every cache-active Claude call
confirmed.claim: Anthropic usage semantics: input_tokens already EXCLUDES cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens (they are reported separately). cost_tracker.record computes regular_input = max(0, input_tokens - cache_read - cache_creation), subtracting them a second time -- whenever cached prefix tokens exceed the uncached remainder (the normal case: ~5K-token cached system vs a few hundred uncached user tokens) regular_input clamps to 0 and the uncached input is priced at $0, under-reporting per-analysis Claude cost. Distinct from CONTESTED register item sovereign_api.py:261 (which omits cache pricing entirely in a different consumer); this is the per-analysis tracker the UI cost cards read.
confirmed.evidence:
cost_tracker.py:175-179:
        if cache_read > 0 or cache_creation > 0:
            regular_input = max(0, input_tokens - cache_read - cache_creation)
            cached_read_cost = cache_read * pricing[0] * 0.1 / 1_000_000
            cache_write_cost = cache_creation * pricing[0] * 2.0 / 1_000_000
            regular_cost = regular_input * pricing[0] / 1_000_000
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T6 rule 6 (token/cost accounting correctness) + Anthropic prompt-caching usage-field semantics (input_tokens = tokens neither read from nor written to cache); claude-api reference confirms the three fields are disjoint.
confirmed.suggested_fix: regular_input = input_tokens (no subtraction) for Anthropic-shaped UsageMeta; add a unit test with input=1000/cache_read=5000 asserting the 1000 uncached tokens are priced.
confirmed.verify_reason: Confirmed. Anthropic usage semantics (verified via claude-api reference: "input_tokens is the uncached remainder only; total = input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens") mean the three fields are disjoint. llm_client.py:1767 maps raw usage.input_tokens into UsageMeta.prompt_token_count with no summing, and cache fields are populated only on that Anthropic path (Gemini path leaves them 0), so cost_tracker.py:176's regular_input = max(0, input_tokens - cache_read - cache_creation) subtracts cache tokens a second time. With the deliberate ~4500-5000-token _HOUSE_INSTRUCTIONS cached prefix (llm_client.py:48-64), cache_read routinely exceeds the uncached remainder, clamping regular_input to 0 and pricing all uncached input at $0 on every cache-active Claude call. Not neutralized by any caller (orchestrator.py:827/1010 pass the response straight through). Origin traced: harness_log.md MF-48 deliberately introduced the double subtraction on wrong field semantics — a latent bug, not project intent. No duplicate in phase-69-73 registers; the sovereign_api.py:261 CONTESTED item is a distinct consumer/defect. P3 is honest: nominal-cost telemetry under-report (fractions of a cent per call on cost cards already flagged NOMINAL), not live trading correctness.

confirmed.title: paper_trader.mark_to_market is a serial N+1: per-position yfinance HTTP fetch + 2 BigQuery DML jobs, executed up to 3x per daily cycle
confirmed.claim: For each open position, mark_to_market serially runs _get_live_price (one `yf.Ticker(t).history(period='1d')` HTTPS round-trip each) then `bq.delete_paper_position(ticker)` (DELETE DML query job) then `_safe_save_position` -> save_paper_position (MERGE DML query job). autonomous_loop's own comment quantifies it: "~14 positions x (yfinance + 2 BQ DML) = 42 blocking" calls — and run_daily_cycle invokes mark_to_market up to three times (autonomous_loop.py:1231, 1271, 1539), i.e. ~126 serial network operations and ~84 BQ DML jobs per cycle, each DML paying ~1-2s job latency. yfinance supports one batched `yf.download([tickers], period='1d')` for all positions, and BigQuery supports a single multi-row MERGE — the per-row loop is pure wall-time and job-quota waste on the money path. (Distinct from the phase-69 register's DELETE-before-MERGE atomicity row: that is a data-loss defect; this is the batching/latency/cost defect, and a batched single-MERGE fix would incidentally retire the delete+insert pattern.)

confirmed.title: Quant-optimizer feature cache is dead code: set/cleared/logged but never read or populated by the engine
confirmed.claim: The optimizer 'activates' feature caching (set_cached_features({}) at :219/:601), hashes data-affecting params, and on ML-only param changes logs "reusing cached features" — but repo-wide grep shows `_cached_features` is referenced ONLY at its setter/getter/clearer (backtest_engine.py:250-260) and these optimizer call sites; no code in _build_training_data/_predict_and_trade (or anywhere else, including tests) ever reads or populates it. Every optimizer iteration therefore rebuilds every feature vector from scratch — thousands of build_feature_vector calls per window, each including a 1000-path x 126-day Monte Carlo GBM simulation (historical_data.py:392-408) — even when only n_estimators/learning_rate changed, while the log claims reuse. The entire cache-key subsystem (_compute_feature_cache_key/_setup_feature_cache/_DATA_AFFECTING_PARAMS) is dead weight with actively misleading observability.

confirmed.title: preload_macro stale-refusal degrades to ~200 per-cutoff BigQuery window-function fallback queries per backtest — which still serve the same stale macro data

Critically, the fallback applies NO staleness check, so the backtest is still powered by exactly the stale FRED values the guard refused to cache — the phase-25.D7 protection ("prevents silently powering backtests with stale macro factors") buys no protection, only a ~200x query amplification and one WARNING line.

Not in the phase-69 register or 72/73/74 queues; the historical_macro FREEZE (contract_75.0.md) makes the guard fire on every current backtest, so the degraded path is the live default. confirmed.title: _compute_sample_weights is an O(n^2) pure-Python double loop over training samples, re-run per window per optimizer iteration
confirmed.claim: Average-uniqueness weights are computed with a nested Python for-loop doing pairwise pd.Timestamp interval-overlap comparisons: n≈1300+ samples per window (26 biweekly dates x ~50 candidates) → ~1.7M+ Python-level iterations per window, growing quadratically as top_n_candidates or the training span increases (walk-forward windows and the biweekly sampling both scale n). At ~8 windows per backtest and one backtest per optimizer iteration this burns seconds-to-tens-of-seconds of pure interpreter time per iteration for a computation that vectorizes trivially (entry/exit arrays + numpy broadcasting, or a sorted-events sweep in O(n log n)).
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md role charter (O(n^2) scans on growing data) + .claude/rules/backend-backtest.md (sample weights via average uniqueness is a per-window training-path step; López de Prado Ch. 4).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Vectorize: convert entry/exit to int64 numpy arrays and compute `overlaps = (entry[None,:] < exit[:,None]) & (exit[None,:] > entry[:,None])`, subtract the diagonal, `weights = 1/(1+overlaps.sum(1))` — identical output, ~100x faster; or an O(n log n) sweep if n grows past ~10k.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verbatim O(n^2) pure-Python double loop confirmed at backtest_engine.py:795-805, comparing pd.Timestamp pairs from Python lists (entry/exit appended at lines 681-684). Called unguarded from _build_training_data (line 738) once per window per backtest per optimizer iteration. Scale claim checks out: biweekly sampling (~26/ticker/yr, line 641 comment) x top_n_candidates=50 (line 164) gives n~1300 per 1-yr span, and expanding walk-forward windows grow n linearly with train span, so cost grows quadratically. Not a duplicate: the only register mention of _compute_sample_weights (phase-69 confirmed.json) was the test-overlap purge leakage item, fixed in 69.2 and orthogonal to perf; no phase-70/71/72/73 item covers this. Rules mandate average-uniqueness weights but not this implementation; the suggested broadcast vectorization is output-identical including strict-inequality overlap and sum-to-n normalization (lines 808-809). P3 honest: pure perf polish, measurable mainly on warm-cache optimizer iterations (<30s target), dominated by feature building on cold runs.

confirmed.title: peer_leadlag screen fetches yf.Ticker().info one ticker at a time with serial awaits (flag-gated)
confirmed.claim: When peer_leadlag_enabled is on, the cycle loops over ~2x paper_screen_top_n candidates and serially awaits one asyncio.to_thread yfinance .info fetch per ticker — each a 0.5-2s+ HTTPS round-trip — adding ~10-40s of dead serial wall-time to every daily cycle. The repo already solved this exact problem in paper_trading._fetch_ticker_meta with a parallel ThreadPoolExecutor, whose comment explicitly says it replaces 'the previous serial loop with sleep(0.3)' and notes Ticker.info is thread-safe — this site regressed to the known-bad pattern. Default OFF flag keeps the live impact latent, hence P3.
confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md role charter (N+1 API call patterns in loops) + the repo's own documented parallel-pool convention at paper_trading.py:1144-1166 (internal best-practice drift).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Fan out: `infos = await asyncio.gather(*[asyncio.to_thread(lambda x=t: yf.Ticker(x).info or {}) for t in target_tickers], return_exceptions=True)` with a small bound, or reuse the existing _fetch_ticker_meta thread-pool helper extended with the two extra fields.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified: autonomous_loop.py:639-647 serially awaits one asyncio.to_thread yf.Ticker(t).info fetch per ticker for 2*paper_screen_top_n (~20, default top_n=10 at settings.py:367) candidates when peer_leadlag_enabled is on — ~10-40s serial wall-time added to the cycle. The repo's own parallel convention exists at backend/api/paper_trading.py:1144-1156 (_fetch_ticker_meta, ThreadPoolExecutor max_workers=5, comment explicitly replacing 'the previous serial loop with sleep(0.3)' and citing Ticker.info thread-safety, measured 18s->3s for 14 tickers), and autonomous_loop itself reuses that helper at :879-888 and :1365-1377 — so this site is genuine best-practice drift. No guard neutralizes it (per-ticker try/except only), not fixed, and no phase-69/70/71/72/73 register duplicate (peer_leadlag register mentions cover only sign-safe overlay math). Flag defaults False (settings.py:514) and the cost lands in a background daily batch job, so P3 latent-perf-polish is the honest severity.

confirmed.title: BigQueryClient is re-constructed per call site (34 sites, no singleton): repeated credential JSON parsing and fresh connection pools
confirmed.claim: Every `BigQueryClient(settings)` re-parses the service-account JSON (`json.loads(settings.gcp_credentials_json)`), rebuilds Credentials, and constructs a brand-new google.cloud.bigquery.Client with its own HTTP session/connection pool. There are 34 construction sites across backend/api/ + backend/services/ — most API endpoints build one per cache-miss request (e.g. paper_trading.py:303, 750, 771, 988, 1008; performance_api.py:59 per poll), and reports.py's `_get_bq` Depends constructs per request with no caching. This forfeits connection/keep-alive reuse to BigQuery (extra TLS + auth-token churn on first query per client) and adds ~50-200ms of constant overhead per construction; the google Cloud Client Libraries guidance is to create clients once and reuse them.

reports.py:24: `def _get_bq(settings: Settings = Depends(get_settings)) -> BigQueryClient:`

confirmed.basis: handoff/current/research_brief_75.0.md role charter (per-call client re-construction is an explicit audit item) + T4 canonical BigQuery client guidance (brief #4/S8 family; Google Cloud client-library best practice: instantiate clients once and reuse — https://cloud.google.com/apis/docs/client-libraries-best-practices).

The underlying bigquery.Client is thread-safe for reuse.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified: bigquery_client.py:22-35 re-parses credential JSON and constructs a fresh google.cloud.bigquery.Client (new HTTP session/pool) on every BigQueryClient(settings) call; grep confirms exactly 34 construction sites in backend/api/ + backend/services/ (49 repo-wide), including per-request sites paper_trading.py:82-1229 (~20 sites), performance_api.py:59 per poll, and reports.py:24-25 _get_bq Depends with no caching. No lru_cache/singleton/factory exists anywhere for BigQueryClient (only get_settings() is cached); alt_data's separate _get_bq_client helpers also construct fresh, so nothing neutralizes the claim. Not a duplicate: phase-69 register references BigQueryClient only for autonomous_loop:167 lock-bricking and the non-atomic upsert at bigquery_client.py:557, not client re-construction; harness_proposals.json has no singleton item. confirmed.title: No Python lockfile: floors have silently drifted majors (pandas 3.x, yfinance 1.x) and pip-audit never scans the real runtime env
confirmed.claim: backend/requirements.txt mixes 9 == pins with ~28 open >= floors and there is no lockfile/constraints file anywhere (find for uv.lock/poetry.lock/Pipfile.lock/requirements*.lock returns only frontend/package-lock.json). The live .venv has drifted across MAJOR versions relative to what the floors were written for (pandas 3.0.1 vs >=2.2.0, numpy 2.4.4, yfinance 1.2.0 vs >=0.2.40, fastapi 0.135.2, pytest 9.0.3, celery 5.6.2), so any fresh `pip install -r` produces a different, untested resolution than the running system. Compounding: .github/workflows/pip-audit.yml audits `--requirement backend/requirements.txt` (a hypothetical fresh resolution), so the ACTUAL installed set — including ~100 venv-only packages (torch 2.12.0, transformers 5.11.0, langchain 1.2.15, litellm 1.83.0, playwright 1.58.0) — is never vulnerability-scanned by anything.
confirmed.evidence: backend/requirements.txt:2-4 'fastapi>=0.115.0 / uvicorn[standard]>=0.30.0 / pydantic>=2.0'; :12 'google-cloud-bigquery>=3.20.0'; :18-19 'yfinance>=0.2.40 / pandas>=2.2.0'. .venv/bin/pip list (2026-07-19): 'pandas 3.0.1', 'numpy 2.4.4', 'yfinance 1.2.0', 'fastapi 0.135.2', 'pytest 9.0.3', 'celery 5.6.2', plus undeclared 'torch 2.12.0', 'litellm 1.83.0', 'langchain 1.2.15'. .github/workflows/pip-audit.yml:47-48 'pip-audit \ --requirement backend/requirements.txt'. requirements.txt:39 comment records the phase-67.6 incident: 'the stale pin was a downgrade time bomb via pip install -r'.
confirmed.basis: research_brief_75.0.md T9 rule 2 ('Backend: no lockfile mechanism at all — introduce uv pip compile ... the .venv is currently the only de-facto lock and is unversioned') + T2 packaging section; official uv docs https://docs.astral.sh/uv/pip/compile/ ('uv pip compile → pinned lock; uv pip sync for exact reproduction').
confirmed.suggested_fix: Generate and commit `uv pip compile backend/requirements.txt -o backend/requirements.lock --generate-hashes` on the repo's platform; document `uv pip sync` (or `pip install -r backend/requirements.lock`) as the reproduction path; point pip-audit.yml at the lock (and optionally add a `pip freeze`-diff drift check against the venv) so the audited graph equals the deployed graph.
confirmed.verify_reason: All evidence verified in-repo: backend/requirements.txt:2 'fastapi>=0.115.0' with 9 == pins vs ~25 open >= floors (claim's ~28 is minor drift); zero lockfile/constraints files anywhere (find confirms; root requirements.txt is a pointer stub); .venv/bin/pip list matches every quoted drifted version exactly (pandas 3.0.1, yfinance 1.2.0, numpy 2.4.4, fastapi 0.135.2, pytest 9.0.3, celery 5.6.2) plus undeclared torch 2.12.0/litellm 1.83.0/langchain 1.2.15/transformers 5.11.0 among 305 installed packages vs ~37 declared; pip-audit.yml:44-48 audits only '--requirement backend/requirements.txt' (fresh ubuntu/py3.14 resolution), so the actual darwin runtime env is never CVE-scanned by any workflow; requirements.txt:39 records the phase-67.6 downgrade-time-bomb incident proving the failure class is real. No guard or wrapper neutralizes it; no pending masterplan step or phase-69/70/71/72/73 register item covers a lockfile (phase-69 'lockfile' hits are the unrelated cycle_lock pidfile; research_brief_75.0.md T9 is this audit's own basis, not a prior register). P1 honest: latent-serious (rebuild produces untested cross-major resolution; high-CVE packages torch/litellm/langchain structurally unscanned), not P0 since the live process is currently functional.

confirmed.title: Runtime code imports exchange_calendars and gpt-researcher, which exist only in the venv and are not declared anywhere

For gpt-researcher, add a separate committed `scripts/autoresearch/requirements-autoresearch.txt` (pinned) so its ~80-package closure (torch/langchain/litellm/etc.) is declared and pip-audit-scannable without bloating the trading backend's manifest; wire run_nightly.sh to fail loudly (page) on ImportError.
confirmed.verify_reason: Verified: exchange_calendars (4.13.2) and gpt-researcher (0.14.8) exist only in .venv; neither appears in backend/requirements.txt or root requirements.txt (which is just '-r backend/requirements.txt'), and a METADATA scan of all venv dist-infos shows no declared package pulls either in transitively. All three exchange_calendars import sites match (markets.py:11-14 try/except -> xcals=None; monthly_champion_challenger.py late import whose own docstring admits the fallback mishandles holiday-shifted last sessions; signal_reliability_test.py:67 ImportError->pass), so a fresh pip rebuild silently changes champion-challenger anchor-Friday logic and intl calendar handling with zero errors. run_memo.py late-imports gpt_researcher and is invoked nightly by launchd via run_nightly.sh (real spend resumed 2026-07-07), which on ImportError only appends FAIL to handoff/autoresearch.log — no page — so the memo pipeline dries up quietly. No fix or pin exists, nothing in CLAUDE.md/.claude/rules declares these optional, and no phase-69/70/71/72/73 register item covers it.
